13	
14	package images
15	
16	import (
17		"errors"
18		"fmt"
19		"image"
20		"image/color"
21		"image/draw"
22		"io"
23		"sync"
24	
25		"github.com/bep/imagemeta"
26		"github.com/bep/logg"
27		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config"
28		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/internal/warpc"
29	
30		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/media"
31		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/images/meta"
32	
33		"github.com/gohugoio/gift"
34	
35		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/himage"
36		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hugio"
37	)
38	
39	func NewImage(f Format, proc *ImageProcessor, img image.Image, s Spec) *Image {
40		if img != nil {
41			return &Image{
42				Format: f,
43				Proc:   proc,
44				Spec:   s,
45				imageConfig: &imageConfig{
46					config:       imageConfigFromImage(img),
47					configLoaded: true,
48				},
49			}
50		}
51		return &Image{Format: f, Proc: proc, Spec: s, imageConfig: &imageConfig{}}
52	}
53	
54	type Image struct {
55		Format Format
56		Proc   *ImageProcessor
57		Spec   Spec
58		*imageConfig
59	}
60	
61	func (i *Image) EncodeTo(conf ImageConfig, img image.Image, w io.Writer) error {
62		return i.Proc.Codec.EncodeTo(conf, w, img)
63	}
64	
65	// Height returns i's height.
66	func (i *Image) Height() int {
67		i.initConfig()
68		return i.config.Height
69	}
70	
71	// Width returns i's width.
72	func (i *Image) Width() int {
73		i.initConfig()
74		return i.config.Width
75	}
76	
77	func (i Image) WithImage(img image.Image) *Image {
78		i.Spec = nil
79		i.imageConfig = &imageConfig{
80			config:       imageConfigFromImage(img),
81			configLoaded: true,
82		}
83	
84		return &i
85	}
86	
87	func (i Image) WithSpec(s Spec) *Image {
88		i.Spec = s
89		i.imageConfig = &imageConfig{}
90		return &i
91	}
92	
93	// InitConfig reads the image config from the given reader.
94	func (i *Image) InitConfig(r io.Reader) error {
95		var err error
96		i.configInit.Do(func() {
97			i.config, _, err = i.Proc.Codec.DecodeConfig(i.Format, r)
98		})
99		return err
100	}
101	
102	func (i *Image) initConfig() {
103		var err error
104		i.configInit.Do(func() {
105			if i.configLoaded {
106				return
107			}
108	
109			var f hugio.ReadSeekCloser
110	
111			f, err = i.Spec.ReadSeekCloser()
112			if err != nil {
113				return
114			}
115			defer f.Close()
116	
117			i.config, _, err = i.Proc.Codec.DecodeConfig(i.Format, f)
118		})
119	
120		if err != nil {
121			panic(fmt.Errorf("failed to load image config: %w", err))
122		}
123	}
124	
125	func NewImageProcessor(debugl, warnl logg.LevelLogger, wasmDispatchers *warpc.Dispatchers, cfg *config.ConfigNamespace[ImagingConfig, ImagingConfigInternal]) (*ImageProcessor, error) {
126		e := cfg.Config.Imaging.Exif
127		exifDecoder, err := meta.NewDecoder(
128			meta.WithDateDisabled(e.DisableDate),
129			meta.WithLatLongDisabled(e.DisableLatLong),
130			meta.ExcludeFields(e.ExcludeFields),
131			meta.IncludeFields(e.IncludeFields),
132			meta.WithWarnLogger(warnl),
133		)
134		if err != nil {
135			return nil, err
136		}
137	
138		m := cfg.Config.Imaging.Meta
139		metaDecoder, err := meta.NewDecoder(
140			meta.WithFields(m.Fields),
141			meta.WithSources(m.Sources...),
142			meta.WithWarnLogger(warnl),
143		)
144		if err != nil {
145			return nil, err
146		}
147	
148		webpCodec, err := wasmDispatchers.NewWepCodec()
149		if err != nil {
150			return nil, err
151		}
152		if webpCodec == nil {
153			return nil, errors.New("webp codec is not available")
154		}
155		avifCodec, err := wasmDispatchers.NewAvifCodec()
156		if err != nil {
157			return nil, err
158		}
159		imageCodec := newCodec(webpCodec, avifCodec, debugl)
160	
161		return &ImageProcessor{
162			Cfg:         cfg,
163			exifDecoder: exifDecoder,
164			metaDecoder: metaDecoder,
165			Codec:       imageCodec,
166		}, nil
167	}
168	
169	type ImageProcessor struct {
170		Cfg         *config.ConfigNamespace[ImagingConfig, ImagingConfigInternal]
171		exifDecoder *meta.Decoder
172		metaDecoder *meta.Decoder
173		Codec       *Codec
174	}
175	
176	// Filename is only used for logging.
177	func (p *ImageProcessor) DecodeExif(filename string, format imagemeta.ImageFormat, r io.Reader) (*meta.ExifInfo, error) {
178		return p.exifDecoder.Decode(filename, format, r)
179	}
180	
181	// DecodeMeta decodes metadata from configured sources.
182	// Filename is only used for logging.
183	func (p *ImageProcessor) DecodeMeta(filename string, format imagemeta.ImageFormat, r io.Reader) (*meta.MetaInfo, error) {
184		return p.metaDecoder.DecodeMeta(filename, format, r)
185	}
186	
187	func (p *ImageProcessor) FiltersFromConfig(src image.Image, conf ImageConfig) ([]gift.Filter, error) {
188		var filters []gift.Filter
189	
190		if conf.Rotate != 0 {
191			// Apply any rotation before any resize.
192			filters = append(filters, gift.Rotate(float32(conf.Rotate), color.Transparent, gift.NearestNeighborInterpolation))
193		}
194	
195		switch conf.Action {
196		case "resize":
197			filters = append(filters, gift.Resize(conf.Width, conf.Height, conf.Filter))
198		case "crop":
199			if conf.Anchor == SmartCropAnchor {
200				bounds, err := p.smartCrop(src, conf.Width, conf.Height, conf.Filter)
201				if err != nil {
202					return nil, err
203				}
204	
205				// First crop using the bounds returned by smartCrop.
206				filters = append(filters, gift.Crop(bounds))
207				// Then center crop the image to get an image the desired size without resizing.
208				filters = append(filters, gift.CropToSize(conf.Width, conf.Height, gift.CenterAnchor))
209	
210			} else {
211				filters = append(filters, gift.CropToSize(conf.Width, conf.Height, conf.Anchor))
212			}
213		case "fill":
214			if conf.Anchor == SmartCropAnchor {
215				bounds, err := p.smartCrop(src, conf.Width, conf.Height, conf.Filter)
216				if err != nil {
217					return nil, err
218				}
219	
220				// First crop it, then resize it.
221				filters = append(filters, gift.Crop(bounds))
222				filters = append(filters, gift.Resize(conf.Width, conf.Height, conf.Filter))
223	
224			} else {
225				filters = append(filters, gift.ResizeToFill(conf.Width, conf.Height, conf.Filter, conf.Anchor))
226			}
227		case "fit":
228			filters = append(filters, gift.ResizeToFit(conf.Width, conf.Height, conf.Filter))
229		default:
230	
231		}
232		return filters, nil
233	}
234	
235	func (p *ImageProcessor) ApplyFiltersFromConfig(src image.Image, conf ImageConfig) (image.Image, error) {
236		filters, err := p.FiltersFromConfig(src, conf)
237		if err != nil {
238			return nil, err
239		}
240	
241		if len(filters) == 0 {
242			return p.resolveSrc(src, conf.TargetFormat), nil
243		}
244	
245		img, err := p.doFilter(src, conf.TargetFormat, filters...)
246		if err != nil {
247			return nil, err
248		}
249	
250		return img, nil
251	}
252	
253	func (p *ImageProcessor) Filter(src image.Image, filters ...gift.Filter) (image.Image, error) {
254		return p.doFilter(src, 0, filters...)
255	}
256	
257	func (p *ImageProcessor) resolveSrc(src image.Image, targetFormat Format) image.Image {
258		if animatedImage, ok := src.(himage.AnimatedImage); ok {
259			frames := animatedImage.GetFrames()
260			// If e.g. converting an animated GIF to JPEG, we only want the first frame.
261			// Preserve the AnimatedImage wrapper for color properties only when there is
262			// no actual animation to preserve. Multi-frame animated sources must still be
263			// collapsed unless the target format supports animation output.
264			shouldExtractFirstFrame := len(frames) < 2 || !targetFormat.SupportsAnimation()
265			colorPropsNeedPreserving := himage.HasColorProperties(src) && len(frames) < 2
266			if shouldExtractFirstFrame && !colorPropsNeedPreserving {
267				src = frames[0]
268			}
269		}
270		return src
271	}
272	
273	func (p *ImageProcessor) doFilter(src image.Image, targetFormat Format, filters ...gift.Filter) (image.Image, error) {
274		filter := gift.New(filters...)
275	
276		if anim, ok := src.(himage.AnimatedImage); ok {
277			frames := anim.GetFrames()
278			// Check if we should extract first frame or preserve AnimatedImage wrapper.
279			// Preserve AnimatedImage if color properties need to be maintained.
280			shouldExtractFirstFrame := len(frames) < 2 || !targetFormat.SupportsAnimation()
281			colorPropsNeedPreserving := himage.HasColorProperties(src) && len(frames) < 2
282			if shouldExtractFirstFrame && !colorPropsNeedPreserving {
283				src = frames[0]
284			} else {
285				var bounds image.Rectangle
286				firstFrame := frames[0]
287				// Preserve bit depth for HDR images.
288				var tmp draw.Image
289				switch firstFrame.(type) {
290				case *image.RGBA64, *image.NRGBA64:
291					tmp = image.NewNRGBA64(firstFrame.Bounds())
292				default:
293					tmp = image.NewNRGBA(firstFrame.Bounds())
294				}
295				for i, frame := range frames {
296					gift.New().DrawAt(tmp, frame, frame.Bounds().Min, gift.OverOperator)
297					bounds = filter.Bounds(tmp.Bounds())
298					var dst draw.Image
299					switch f := frame.(type) {
300					case *image.Paletted:
301						// Gif.
302						dst = image.NewPaletted(bounds, f.Palette)
303					case *image.RGBA64:
304						dst = image.NewRGBA64(bounds)
305					case *image.NRGBA64:
306						dst = image.NewNRGBA64(bounds)
307					default:
308						dst = image.NewNRGBA(bounds)
309					}
310					filter.Draw(dst, tmp)
311					frames[i] = dst
312				}
313				anim.SetWidthHeight(bounds.Dx(), bounds.Dy())
314				anim.SetFrames(frames)
315	
316				return anim, nil
317			}
318	
319		}
320	
321		bounds := filter.Bounds(src.Bounds())
322	
323		var dst draw.Image
324		switch src.(type) {
325		case *image.RGBA:
326			dst = image.NewRGBA(bounds)
327		case *image.NRGBA:
328			dst = image.NewNRGBA(bounds)
329		case *image.Gray:
330			dst = image.NewGray(bounds)
331		case *image.RGBA64:
332			dst = image.NewRGBA64(bounds)
333		case *image.NRGBA64:
334			dst = image.NewNRGBA64(bounds)
335		case *image.Gray16:
336			dst = image.NewGray16(bounds)
337		default:
338			dst = image.NewNRGBA(bounds)
339		}
340		filter.Draw(dst, src)
341	
342		return dst, nil
343	}
344	
345	func newImageConfig() ImageConfig {
346		return ImageConfig{
347			Anchor: -1, // The real values start at 0.
348		}
349	}
350	
351	type Spec interface {
352		// Loads the image source.
353		ReadSeekCloser() (hugio.ReadSeekCloser, error)
354	}
355	
356	// Format is an image file format.
357	type Format int
358	
359	const (
360		JPEG Format = iota + 1
361		PNG
362		GIF
363		TIFF
364		BMP
365		WEBP
366		AVIF
367	
368		// Below: We have no encoder/decoder for these, but we can provide metadata support for them (including width/height).
369		HEIF
370		HEIC
371	)
372	
373	// Whether to use imagemeta to decode image config (width/height	).
374	func (f Format) UseImageMetaConfigDecoder() bool {
375		return f == WEBP || f == AVIF || f == HEIF || f == HEIC
376	}
377	
378	func (f Format) ToImageMetaImageFormatFormat() imagemeta.ImageFormat {
379		switch f {
380		case JPEG:
381			return imagemeta.JPEG
382		case PNG:
383			return imagemeta.PNG
384		case TIFF:
385			return imagemeta.TIFF
386		case WEBP:
387			return imagemeta.WebP
388		case AVIF:
389			return imagemeta.AVIF
390		case HEIF:
391			return imagemeta.HEIF
392		case HEIC:
393			return imagemeta.HEIF
394		default:
395			return -1
396		}
397	}
398	
399	// SupportsTransparency reports whether it supports transparency in any form.
400	func (f Format) SupportsTransparency() bool {
401		return f != JPEG
402	}
403	
404	// SupportsAnimation reports whether the format supports animation.
405	// AVIF supports animation in the spec, but Hugo's AVIF encoder is single-frame
406	// only — including AVIF here would make the pipeline preserve frames just to
407	// drop them at encode time.
408	func (f Format) SupportsAnimation() bool {
409		return f == GIF || f == WEBP
410	}
411	
412	// DefaultExtension returns the default file extension of this format, starting with a dot.
413	// For example: .jpg for JPEG
414	func (f Format) DefaultExtension() string {
415		return f.MediaType().FirstSuffix.FullSuffix
416	}
417	
418	// MediaType returns the media type of this image, e.g. image/jpeg for JPEG
419	func (f Format) MediaType() media.Type {
420		switch f {
421		case JPEG:
422			return media.Builtin.JPEGType
423		case PNG:
424			return media.Builtin.PNGType
425		case GIF:
426			return media.Builtin.GIFType
427		case TIFF:
428			return media.Builtin.TIFFType
429		case BMP:
430			return media.Builtin.BMPType
431		case WEBP:
432			return media.Builtin.WEBPType
433		case AVIF:
434			return media.Builtin.AVIFType
435		case HEIF:
436			return media.Builtin.HEIFType
437		case HEIC:
438			return media.Builtin.HEICType
439		default:
440			panic(fmt.Sprintf("%d is not a valid image format", f))
441		}
442	}
443	
444	func (f Format) String() string {
445		switch f {
446		case JPEG:
447			return "JPEG"
448		case PNG:
449			return "PNG"
450		case GIF:
451			return "GIF"
452		case TIFF:
453			return "TIFF"
454		case BMP:
455			return "BMP"
456		case WEBP:
457			return "WEBP"
458		case AVIF:
459			return "AVIF"
460		case HEIF:
461			return "HEIF"
462		case HEIC:
463			return "HEIC"
464		default:
465			return "Unknown"
466		}
467	}
468	
469	type imageConfig struct {
470		config       image.Config
471		configInit   sync.Once
472		configLoaded bool
473	}
474	
475	func imageConfigFromImage(img image.Image) image.Config {
476		if cp, ok := img.(himage.ImageConfigProvider); ok {
477			return cp.GetImageConfig()
478		}
479		b := img.Bounds()
480		return image.Config{Width: b.Max.X, Height: b.Max.Y}
481	}
482	
483	// UnwrapFilter unwraps the given filter if it is a filter wrapper.
484	func UnwrapFilter(in gift.Filter) gift.Filter {
485		if f, ok := in.(filter); ok {
486			return f.Filter
487		}
488		return in
489	}
490	
491	// ToFilters converts the given input to a slice of gift.Filter.
492	func ToFilters(in any) []gift.Filter {
493		switch v := in.(type) {
494		case []gift.Filter:
495			return v
496		case []filter:
497			vv := make([]gift.Filter, len(v))
498			for i, f := range v {
499				vv[i] = f
500			}
501			return vv
502		case gift.Filter:
503			return []gift.Filter{v}
504		default:
505			panic(fmt.Sprintf("%T is not an image filter", in))
506		}
507	}
508	
509	// IsOpaque returns false if the image has alpha channel and there is at least 1
510	// pixel that is not (fully) opaque.
511	func IsOpaque(img image.Image) bool {
512		if oim, ok := img.(interface {
513			Opaque() bool
514		}); ok {
515			return oim.Opaque()
516		}
517	
518		return false
519	}
520	
521	// ImageSource identifies and decodes an image.
522	type ImageSource interface {
523		DecodeImage() (image.Image, error)
524		Key() string
525	}

14	package commands
15	
16	import (
17		"context"
18		"runtime"
19	
20		"github.com/bep/simplecobra"
21		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hugo"
22		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
23	)
24	
25	func newEnvCommand() simplecobra.Commander {
26		return &simpleCommand{
27			name:  "env",
28			short: "Display version and environment info",
29			long:  "Display version and environment info. This is useful in Hugo bug reports",
30			run: func(ctx context.Context, cd *simplecobra.Commandeer, r *rootCommand, args []string) error {
31				r.Printf("%s\n", hugo.BuildVersionString())
32				r.Printf("GOOS=%q\n", runtime.GOOS)
33				r.Printf("GOARCH=%q\n", runtime.GOARCH)
34				r.Printf("GOVERSION=%q\n", runtime.Version())
35	
36				if r.isVerbose() {
37					deps := hugo.GetDependencyList()
38					for _, dep := range deps {
39						r.Printf("%s\n", dep)
40					}
41				} else {
42					// These are also included in the GetDependencyList above;
43					// always print these as these are most likely the most useful to know about.
44					deps := hugo.GetDependencyListNonGo()
45					for _, dep := range deps {
46						r.Printf("%s\n", dep)
47					}
48				}
49				return nil
50			},
51			withc: func(cmd *cobra.Command, r *rootCommand) {
52				cmd.ValidArgsFunction = cobra.NoFileCompletions
53			},
54		}
55	}
56	
57	func newVersionCmd() simplecobra.Commander {
58		return &simpleCommand{
59			name: "version",
60			run: func(ctx context.Context, cd *simplecobra.Commandeer, r *rootCommand, args []string) error {
61				r.Println(hugo.BuildVersionString())
62				return nil
63			},
64			short: "Display version",
65			long:  "Display version and environment info. This is useful in Hugo bug reports.",
66			withc: func(cmd *cobra.Command, r *rootCommand) {
67				cmd.ValidArgsFunction = cobra.NoFileCompletions
68			},
69		}
70	}

95	
96	type siteState int
97	
98	const (
99		siteStateInit siteState = iota
100		siteStateReady
101	)
102	
103	type Site struct {
104		state       siteState
105		conf        *allconfig.Config
106		language    *langs.Language
107		store       *hstore.Scratch
108		siteWrapped page.Site
109	
110		// The owning container.
111		h *HugoSites
112	
113		*deps.Deps
114		*siteLanguageVersionRole
115	
116		relatedDocsHandler *page.RelatedDocsHandler
117	
118		publisher          publisher.Publisher
119		frontmatterHandler pagemeta.FrontMatterHandler
120	}
121	
122	func (s Site) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) (*Site, error) {
123		s.siteLanguageVersionRole = s.siteLanguageVersionRole.cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role)
124		d, err := s.Deps.Clone(&s, s.Conf)
125		if err != nil {
126			return nil, err
127		}
128		s.Deps = d
129		ss := &s
130		ss.siteWrapped = page.WrapSite(ss)
131		return ss, nil
132	}
133	
134	// For debugging purposes only.
135	func (s *Site) resolveDimensionNames() types.Strings3 {
136		return s.Conf.ConfiguredDimensions().ResolveNames(s.siteVector)
137	}
138	
139	type siteLanguageVersionRole struct {
140		siteVector        sitesmatrix.Vector
141		isDefaultLanguage bool
142	
143		roleInternal roles.RoleInternal
144		role         roles.Role
145	
146		versionInternal versions.VersionInternal
147		version         versions.Version
148	
149		pageMap *pageMap
150	
151		// Page navigation.
152		*pageFinder
153		siteRefLinker siteRefLinker
154	
155		// Shortcut to the home page. Note that this may be nil if
156		// home page, for some odd reason, is disabled.
157		home *pageState
158	
159		// The last modification date of this site.
160		lastmod time.Time
161	
162		// Lazily loaded site dependencies
163		init *siteInit
164	
165		// The output formats that we need to render this site in. This slice
166		// will be fixed once set.
167		// This will be the union of Site.Pages' outputFormats.
168		// This slice will be sorted.
169		renderFormats output.Formats
170	}
171	
172	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) *siteLanguageVersionRole {
173		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Version] = version
174		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Role] = role
175		s.home = nil
176		s.lastmod = time.Time{}
177		s.init = &siteInit{}
178		return &s

198	// NewHugoSites creates HugoSites from the given config.
199	func NewHugoSites(cfg deps.DepsCfg) (*HugoSites, error) {
200		if !cfg.TestCfg.IsZero() && !cfg.IsIntegrationTest {
201			panic("DepsCfg.TestCfg must only be set in integration tests")
202		}
203	
204		conf := cfg.Configs.GetFirstLanguageConfig()
205		rolesSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Roles.Config.Sorted
206		versionsSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Versions.Config.Sorted
207	
208		var (
209			poolSizeKatex = 2
210			poolSizeWebP  = 1
211			poolSizeAvif  = 1
212		)
213		if n := config.GetNumWorkerMultiplier(); n > 1 {
214			poolSizeKatex = min(n, 8)
215			poolSizeWebP = max(2, n/2)
216			poolSizeAvif = max(2, n/2)
217		}
218	
219		var logger loggers.Logger
220		if cfg.TestLogger != nil {
221			logger = cfg.TestLogger
222		} else {
223			var logHookLast func(e *logg.Entry) error
224			if cfg.Configs.Base.PanicOnWarning {
225				logHookLast = loggers.PanicOnWarningHook
226			}
227			if cfg.StdOut == nil {
228				cfg.StdOut = os.Stdout
229			}
230			if cfg.StdErr == nil {
231				cfg.StdErr = os.Stderr
232			}
233			if cfg.LogLevel == 0 {
234				cfg.LogLevel = logg.LevelWarn
235			}
236	
237			logOpts := loggers.Options{
238				Level:              cfg.LogLevel,
239				DistinctLevel:      logg.LevelWarn, // This will drop duplicate log warning and errors.
240				HandlerPost:        logHookLast,
241				StdOut:             cfg.StdOut,
242				StdErr:             cfg.StdErr,
243				StoreErrors:        conf.Watching(),
244				SuppressStatements: conf.IgnoredLogs(),
245			}
246			logger = loggers.New(logOpts)
247	
248		}
249	
250		memCache := dynacache.New(dynacache.Options{Watching: conf.Watching(), Log: logger})
251	
252		var h *HugoSites
253		onSignalRebuild := func(ids ...identity.Identity) {
254			// This channel is buffered, but make sure we do this in a non-blocking way.
255			if cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
256				go func() {
257					cfg.ChangesFromBuild <- ids
258				}()
259			}
260		}
261	
262		compilationCacheDir := filepath.Join(conf.Dirs().CacheDir, "_warpc")
263	
264		imageWasmMemory := 384 // 384 MiB (4096 MiB Max)
265		if m := cfg.TestCfg.WarpcMemory; m > 0 {
266			imageWasmMemory = m
267		}
268	
269		firstSiteDeps := &deps.Deps{
270			Fs:   cfg.Fs,
271			Log:  logger,
272			Conf: conf,
273			BuildState: &deps.BuildState{
274				OnSignalRebuild: onSignalRebuild,
275			},
276			Counters:            &deps.Counters{},
277			MemCache:            memCache,
278			TranslationProvider: i18n.NewTranslationProvider(),
279			WasmDispatchers: warpc.AllDispatchers(
280				// Katex options.
281				warpc.Options{
282					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
283	
284					PoolSize: poolSizeKatex,
285					Infof:    logger.InfoCommand("katex").Logf,
286					Warnf:    logger.WarnCommand("katex").Logf,
287				},
288				// WebP options.
289				warpc.Options{
290					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
291					PoolSize:            poolSizeWebP,
292					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
293					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("webp").Logf,
294					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("webp").Logf,
295				},
296				// Avif options.
297				warpc.Options{
298					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
299					PoolSize:            poolSizeAvif,
300					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
301					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("avif").Logf,
302					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("avif").Logf,
303				},
304			),
305		}
306	
307		if err := firstSiteDeps.Init(); err != nil {
308			return nil, err
309		}
310	
311		// Prevent leaking goroutines in tests.
312		if cfg.IsIntegrationTest && cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
313			firstSiteDeps.BuildClosers.Add(types.CloserFunc(func() error {
314				close(cfg.ChangesFromBuild)
315				return nil
316			}))
317		}
318	
319		batcherClient, err := esbuild.NewBatcherClient(firstSiteDeps)
320		if err != nil {
321			return nil, err
322		}
323		firstSiteDeps.JSBatcherClient = batcherClient
324	
325		confm := cfg.Configs
326		if err := confm.Validate(logger); err != nil {
327			return nil, err
328		}
329		var sites []*Site
330	
331		ns := &contentNodeShifter{
332			conf: conf,
333		}
334	
335		treeConfig := doctree.Config[contentNode]{
336			Shifter:        ns,
337			TransformerRaw: &contentNodeTransformerRaw{},
338		}
339	
340		dimensionLengths := sitesmatrix.Vector{len(confm.Languages), len(versionsSorted), len(rolesSorted)}
341	
342		pageTrees := &pageTrees{
343			treePages: doctree.New(
344				treeConfig,
345			),
346			treeResources: doctree.New(
347				treeConfig,
348			),
349			treeTaxonomyEntries:           doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*weightedContentNode](dimensionLengths),
350			treePagesFromTemplateAdapters: doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*pagesfromdata.PagesFromTemplate](dimensionLengths),
351		}
352	
353		pageTrees.createMutableTrees()
354	
355		for i, confp := range confm.ConfigLangs() {
356			language := confp.Language().(*langs.Language)
357			if language.Disabled {
358				panic("cannot create site for disabled language: " + language.Lang)
359			}
360			k := language.Lang
361			conf := confm.LanguageConfigMap[k]
362			frontmatterHandler, err := pagemeta.NewFrontmatterHandler(firstSiteDeps.Log, conf.Frontmatter)
363			if err != nil {
364				return nil, err
365			}
366	
367			langs.SetParams(language, conf.Params)
368	
369			s := &Site{
370				conf:               conf,
371				language:           language,
372				frontmatterHandler: frontmatterHandler,
373				store:              hstore.NewScratch(),
374				siteLanguageVersionRole: &siteLanguageVersionRole{
375					isDefaultLanguage: language.IsDefault(),
376					siteVector:        sitesmatrix.Vector{i, 0, 0},
377				},
378			}
379	
380			s.siteWrapped = page.WrapSite(s)
381	
382			if i == 0 {
383				firstSiteDeps.Site = s
384				s.Deps = firstSiteDeps
385			} else {
386				d, err := firstSiteDeps.Clone(s, confp)
387				if err != nil {
388					return nil, err
389				}
390				s.Deps = d
391			}
392	
393			// Set up the main publishing chain.
394			pub, err := publisher.NewDestinationPublisher(
395				firstSiteDeps.ResourceSpec,
396				s.conf.OutputFormats.Config,
397				s.conf.MediaTypes.Config,
398			)
399			if err != nil {
400				return nil, err
401			}
402	
403			s.publisher = pub
404			s.relatedDocsHandler = page.NewRelatedDocsHandler(s.conf.Related)
405			// Site deps end.
406	
407			sites = append(sites, s)
408	
409		}
410	
411		if len(sites) == 0 {
412			return nil, errors.New("no sites to build")
413		}
414	
415		sitesVersionsRoles := make([][][]*Site, len(sites))
416		for i := 0; i < len(sites); i++ {
417			sitesVersionsRoles[i] = make([][]*Site, len(versionsSorted))
418			for j := range versionsSorted {
419				sitesVersionsRoles[i][j] = make([]*Site, len(rolesSorted))
420			}
421		}
422	
423		siteVersionRoles := map[types.Ints2][]roles.Role{}
424		siteRoleVersions := map[types.Ints2][]versions.Version{}
425		// i = site, j = version, k = role
426		for i, v1 := range sitesVersionsRoles {
427			for j, v2 := range v1 {
428				for k := range v2 {
429					var vrs *Site
430					if j == 0 && k == 0 {
431						vrs = sites[i]
432					} else {
433						prototype := sites[i]
434						vrs, err = prototype.cloneForVersionAndRole(j, k)
435						if err != nil {
436							return nil, err
437						}
438					}
439					vrs.roleInternal = rolesSorted[k]
440					vrs.role = roles.NewRole(rolesSorted[k])
441					vrs.versionInternal = versionsSorted[j]
442					vrs.version = versions.NewVersion(versionsSorted[j])
443					siteRoleVersions[types.Ints2{i, k}] = append(siteRoleVersions[types.Ints2{i, k}], vrs.version)
444					siteVersionRoles[types.Ints2{i, j}] = append(siteVersionRoles[types.Ints2{i, j}], vrs.role)
445					vrs.pageMap = newPageMap(vrs, memCache, pageTrees)
446					vrs.pageFinder = newPageFinder(vrs.pageMap)
447					vrs.siteRefLinker = newSiteRefLinker(vrs)
448					vrs.prepareInits()
449					v2[k] = vrs
450				}
451			}
452		}
453	
454		h, err = newHugoSites(cfg, firstSiteDeps, pageTrees, sitesVersionsRoles)
455		if err == nil && h == nil {
456			panic("hugo: newHugoSitesNew returned nil error and nil HugoSites")
457		}
458	
459		return h, err
460	}
461	
462	func newHugoSites(
463		cfg deps.DepsCfg,
464		d *deps.Deps,
465		pageTrees *pageTrees,
466		sitesVersionsRoles [][][]*Site,
467	) (*HugoSites, error) {
468		first := make([]*Site, len(sitesVersionsRoles))
469		for i, v := range sitesVersionsRoles {
470			first[i] = v[0][0]
471		}
472	
473		sitesVersionsRolesMap := map[sitesmatrix.Vector]*Site{}
474		for _, v1 := range sitesVersionsRoles {
475			for _, v2 := range v1 {
476				for _, s := range v2 {
477					sitesVersionsRolesMap[s.siteVector] = s
478				}
479			}
480		}
481	
482		numWorkers := config.GetNumWorkerMultiplier()
483		numWorkersSite := min(numWorkers, len(sitesVersionsRolesMap))
484		workersSite := para.New(numWorkersSite)
485	
486		var sitesLanguages []*Site
487		for _, v := range sitesVersionsRoles {
488			sitesLanguages = append(sitesLanguages, v[0][0])
489		}
490	
491		h := &HugoSites{
492			Sites:                 first,
493			sitesVersionsRoles:    sitesVersionsRoles,
494			sitesVersionsRolesMap: sitesVersionsRolesMap,
495			sitesLanguages:        sitesLanguages,
496			Deps:                  first[0].Deps,
497			Configs:               cfg.Configs,
498			workersSite:           workersSite,
499			numWorkersSites:       numWorkers,
500			numWorkers:            numWorkers,
501			pageTrees:             pageTrees,
502			cachePages: dynacache.GetOrCreatePartition[string,
503				page.Pages](d.MemCache, "/pags/all",
504				dynacache.OptionsPartition{Weight: 10, ClearWhen: dynacache.ClearOnRebuild},
505			),
506			cacheContentSource:      dynacache.GetOrCreatePartition[uint64, *resources.StaleValue[[]byte]](d.MemCache, "/cont/src", dynacache.OptionsPartition{Weight: 70, ClearWhen: dynacache.ClearOnChange}),
507			translationKeyPages:     hmaps.NewSliceCache[page.Page](),
508			currentSite:             first[0],
509			skipRebuildForFilenames: make(map[string]bool),
510			init:                    &hugoSitesInit{},
511			progressReporter:        &progressReporter{},
512		}
513	
514		// Assemble dependencies to be used in hugo.Deps.
515		var dependencies []*hugo.Dependency
516		var depFromMod func(m modules.Module) *hugo.Dependency
517		depFromMod = func(m modules.Module) *hugo.Dependency {
518			dep := &hugo.Dependency{
519				Path:    m.Path(),
520				Version: m.Version(),
521				Time:    m.Time(),
522				Vendor:  m.Vendor(),
523			}
524	
525			// These are pointers, but this all came from JSON so there's no recursive navigation,
526			// so just create new values.
527			if m.Replace() != nil {
528				dep.Replace = depFromMod(m.Replace())
529			}
530			if m.Owner() != nil {
531				dep.Owner = depFromMod(m.Owner())
532			}
533			return dep
534		}
535		for _, m := range d.Paths.AllModules() {
536			dependencies = append(dependencies, depFromMod(m))
537		}
538	
539		// Create providers that avoid naming conflicts with HugoSites fields.
540		sp := hugoSitesSitesProvider{h: h}
541	
542		opts := page.HugoInfoOptions{
543			Conf:                      h.Configs.GetFirstLanguageConfig(),
544			HugoInfoHugoSitesProvider: sp,
545			Deps:                      dependencies,
546		}
547	
548		if bi := hugo.GetBuildInfo(); bi != nil {
549			opts.CommitHash = bi.Revision
550			opts.BuildDate = bi.RevisionTime
551			opts.GoVersion = bi.GoVersion
552		}
553	
554		if opts.BuildDate == "" {
555			opts.BuildDate = hugo.GetBuildDate()
556		}
557	
558		h.hugoInfo = page.NewHugoInfo(opts)
559	
560		var prototype *deps.Deps
561	
562		var i int
563		for _, v := range sitesVersionsRoles {
564			for _, r := range v {
565				for _, s := range r {
566					s.h = h
567					// The template store needs to be initialized after the h container is set on s.
568					if i == 0 {
569						templateStore, err := tplimpl.NewStore(
570							tplimpl.StoreOptions{
571								Fs:                     s.BaseFs.Layouts.Fs,
572								Log:                    s.Log,
573								Watching:               s.Conf.Watching(),
574								PathParser:             s.Conf.PathParser(),
575								Metrics:                d.Metrics,
576								OutputFormats:          s.conf.OutputFormats.Config,
577								MediaTypes:             s.conf.MediaTypes.Config,
578								DefaultOutputFormat:    s.conf.DefaultOutputFormat,
579								TaxonomySingularPlural: s.conf.Taxonomies,
580								RenderHooks:            s.conf.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks,
581							}, tplimpl.SiteOptions{
582								Site:          s,
583								TemplateFuncs: tplimplinit.CreateFuncMap(s.Deps),
584							})
585						if err != nil {
586							return nil, err
587						}
588						s.Deps.TemplateStore = templateStore
589					} else {
590						s.Deps.TemplateStore = prototype.TemplateStore.WithSiteOpts(
591							tplimpl.SiteOptions{
592								Site:          s,
593								TemplateFuncs: tplimplinit.CreateFuncMap(s.Deps),
594							})
595					}
596					if err := s.Deps.Compile(prototype); err != nil {
597						return nil, err
598					}
599					if i == 0 {
600						prototype = s.Deps
601					}
602	
603					i++
604				}
605			}
606		}
607	
608		h.fatalErrorHandler = &fatalErrorHandler{
609			h:     h,
610			donec: make(chan bool),
611		}
612	
613		h.init.data = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
614			return h.loadData()
615		})
616	
617		h.init.gitInfo = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
618			return h.loadGitInfo()
619		})
620	
621		return h, nil
622	}

624	// GetSiteDimension returns the value of the specified site dimension (such as language, version, or role)
625	// based on the provided dimension name string. If the dimension name is unknown, it panics.
626	func (s *Site) Dimension(d string) page.SiteDimension {
627		switch d {
628		case sitesmatrix.DimensionName(sitesmatrix.Language):
629			return s.language
630		case sitesmatrix.DimensionName(sitesmatrix.Version):
631			return s.version
632		case sitesmatrix.DimensionName(sitesmatrix.Role):
633			return s.role
634		default:
635			panic(fmt.Sprintf("unknown dimension %q", d))
636		}
637	}

819	
820	type (
821		taxonomiesConfig       map[string]string
822		taxonomiesConfigValues struct {
823			views          []viewName
824			viewsByTreeKey map[string]viewName
825		}
826	)
827	
828	func (t taxonomiesConfig) Values() taxonomiesConfigValues {
829		var views []viewName
830		for k, v := range t {
831			views = append(views, viewName{singular: k, plural: v, pluralTreeKey: cleanTreeKey(v)})
832		}
833		sort.Slice(views, func(i, j int) bool {
834			return views[i].plural < views[j].plural
835		})
836	
837		viewsByTreeKey := make(map[string]viewName)
838		for _, v := range views {
839			viewsByTreeKey[v.pluralTreeKey] = v
840		}
841	
842		return taxonomiesConfigValues{
843			views:          views,
844			viewsByTreeKey: viewsByTreeKey,
845		}
846	}

851		prevNextInSection hsync.FuncResetter

864	func (s *Site) prepareInits() {
865		s.init = &siteInit{}
866	
867		s.init.prevNext = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
868			regularPages := s.RegularPages()
869			if s.conf.Page.NextPrevSortOrder == "asc" {
870				regularPages = regularPages.Reverse()
871			}
872			for i, p := range regularPages {
873				np, ok := p.(nextPrevProvider)
874				if !ok {
875					continue
876				}
877	
878				pos := np.getNextPrev()
879				if pos == nil {
880					continue
881				}
882	
883				pos.nextPage = nil
884				pos.prevPage = nil
885	
886				if i > 0 {
887					pos.nextPage = regularPages[i-1]
888				}
889	
890				if i < len(regularPages)-1 {
891					pos.prevPage = regularPages[i+1]
892				}
893			}
894			return nil
895		})
896	
897		s.init.prevNextInSection = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
898			setNextPrev := func(pas page.Pages) {
899				for i, p := range pas {
900					np, ok := p.(nextPrevInSectionProvider)
901					if !ok {
902						continue
903					}
904	
905					pos := np.getNextPrevInSection()
906					if pos == nil {
907						continue
908					}
909	
910					pos.nextPage = nil
911					pos.prevPage = nil
912	
913					if i > 0 {
914						pos.nextPage = pas[i-1]
915					}
916	
917					if i < len(pas)-1 {
918						pos.prevPage = pas[i+1]
919					}
920				}
921			}
922	
923			sections := s.pageMap.getPagesInSection(
924				pageMapQueryPagesInSection{
925					pageMapQueryPagesBelowPath: pageMapQueryPagesBelowPath{
926						Path:    "",
927						KeyPart: "sectionorhome",
928						Include: pagePredicates.KindSection.Or(pagePredicates.KindHome).BoolFunc(),
929					},
930					IncludeSelf: true,
931					Recursive:   true,
932				},
933			)
934	
935			for _, section := range sections {
936				ps := section.RegularPages()
937				if s.conf.Page.NextPrevInSectionSortOrder == "asc" {
938					ps = ps.Reverse()
939				}
940				setNextPrev(ps)
941			}
942	
943			return nil
944		})
945	
946		s.init.menus = hsync.OnceMoreValue(func(ctx context.Context) navigation.Menus {
947			m, err := s.assembleMenus()
948			if err != nil {
949				panic(err)
950			}
951			return m
952		})
953	
954		s.init.taxonomies = hsync.OnceMoreValue(func(ctx context.Context) page.TaxonomyList {
955			taxonomies, err := s.pageMap.CreateSiteTaxonomies(ctx)
956			if err != nil {
957				panic(err)
958			}
959			return taxonomies
960		})
961	}
962	
963	func (s *Site) Menus() navigation.Menus {
964		s.CheckReady()
965		return s.init.menus.Value(context.Background())
966	}

968	func (s *Site) initRenderFormats() {
969		formatSet := make(map[string]bool)
970		formats := output.Formats{}
971	
972		w := &doctree.NodeShiftTreeWalker[contentNode]{
973			Tree: s.pageMap.treePages,
974			Handle: func(key string, n contentNode) (radix.WalkFlag, error) {
975				if p, ok := n.(*pageState); ok {
976					for _, f := range p.m.pageConfig.ConfiguredOutputFormats {
977						if !formatSet[f.Name] {
978							formats = append(formats, f)
979							formatSet[f.Name] = true
980						}
981					}
982				}
983				return radix.WalkContinue, nil
984			},
985		}
986	
987		if err := w.Walk(context.TODO()); err != nil {
988			panic(err)
989		}
990	
991		// Add the per kind configured output formats
992		for _, kind := range kinds.AllKindsInPages {
993			if siteFormats, found := s.conf.C.KindOutputFormats[kind]; found {
994				for _, f := range siteFormats {
995					if !formatSet[f.Name] {
996						formats = append(formats, f)
997						formatSet[f.Name] = true
998					}
999				}
1000			}
1001		}
1002	
1003		sort.Sort(formats)
1004		s.renderFormats = formats
1005	}

1023	type siteRefLinker struct {
1024		s *Site
1025	
1026		errorLogger logg.LevelLogger
1027		notFoundURL string
1028	}
1029	
1030	func newSiteRefLinker(s *Site) siteRefLinker {
1031		logger := s.Log.Error()
1032	
1033		notFoundURL := s.conf.RefLinksNotFoundURL
1034		errLevel := s.conf.RefLinksErrorLevel
1035		if strings.EqualFold(errLevel, "warning") {
1036			logger = s.Log.Warn()
1037		}
1038		return siteRefLinker{s: s, errorLogger: logger, notFoundURL: notFoundURL}
1039	}
1040	
1041	func (s siteRefLinker) logNotFound(ref, what string, p page.Page, position text.Position) {
1042		if position.IsValid() {
1043			s.errorLogger.Logf("[%s] REF_NOT_FOUND: Ref %q: %s: %s", s.s.Lang(), ref, position.String(), what)
1044		} else if p == nil {
1045			s.errorLogger.Logf("[%s] REF_NOT_FOUND: Ref %q: %s", s.s.Lang(), ref, what)
1046		} else {
1047			s.errorLogger.Logf("[%s] REF_NOT_FOUND: Ref %q from page %q: %s", s.s.Lang(), ref, p.Path(), what)
1048		}
1049	}
1050	
1051	func (s *siteRefLinker) refLink(ref string, source any, relative bool, outputFormat string) (string, error) {
1052		p, err := unwrapPage(source)
1053		if err != nil {
1054			return "", err
1055		}
1056	
1057		var refURL *url.URL
1058	
1059		ref = filepath.ToSlash(ref)
1060	
1061		refURL, err = url.Parse(ref)
1062		if err != nil {
1063			return s.notFoundURL, err
1064		}
1065	
1066		var target page.Page
1067		var link string
1068	
1069		if refURL.Path != "" {
1070			var err error
1071			target, err = s.s.getPageRef(p, refURL.Path)
1072			var pos text.Position
1073			if err != nil || target == nil {
1074				if p, ok := source.(text.Positioner); ok {
1075					pos = p.Position()
1076				}
1077			}
1078	
1079			if err != nil {
1080				s.logNotFound(refURL.Path, err.Error(), p, pos)
1081				return s.notFoundURL, nil
1082			}
1083	
1084			if target == nil {
1085				s.logNotFound(refURL.Path, "page not found", p, pos)
1086				return s.notFoundURL, nil
1087			}
1088	
1089			var permalinker Permalinker = target
1090	
1091			if outputFormat != "" {
1092				o := target.OutputFormats().Get(outputFormat)
1093	
1094				if o.IsZero() {
1095					s.logNotFound(refURL.Path, fmt.Sprintf("output format %q", outputFormat), p, pos)
1096					return s.notFoundURL, nil
1097				}
1098				permalinker = o
1099			}
1100	
1101			if relative {
1102				link = permalinker.RelPermalink()
1103			} else {
1104				link = permalinker.Permalink()
1105			}
1106		}
1107	
1108		if refURL.Fragment != "" {
1109			_ = target
1110			link = link + "#" + refURL.Fragment
1111	
1112			if pctx, ok := target.(pageContext); ok {
1113				if refURL.Path != "" {
1114					if di, ok := pctx.getContentConverter().(converter.DocumentInfo); ok {
1115						link = link + di.AnchorSuffix()
1116					}
1117				}
1118			} else if pctx, ok := p.(pageContext); ok {
1119				if di, ok := pctx.getContentConverter().(converter.DocumentInfo); ok {
1120					link = link + di.AnchorSuffix()
1121				}
1122			}
1123	
1124		}
1125	
1126		return link, nil
1127	}

1136		needsPagesAssembly bool

1138		ids map[identity.Identity]bool

1140	
1141	func (w *WhatChanged) init() {
1142		if w.ids == nil {
1143			w.ids = make(map[identity.Identity]bool)
1144		}
1145	}
1146	
1147	func (w *WhatChanged) Add(ids ...identity.Identity) {
1148		w.mu.Lock()
1149		defer w.mu.Unlock()
1150	
1151		w.init()
1152	
1153		for _, id := range ids {
1154			w.ids[id] = true
1155		}
1156	}
1157	
1158	func (w *WhatChanged) Clear() {
1159		w.mu.Lock()
1160		defer w.mu.Unlock()
1161		w.clear()

1166	}

1169		if w == nil || w.ids == nil {
1170			return nil
1171		}
1172		return slices.Collect(maps.Keys(w.ids))
1173	}

1175	func (w *WhatChanged) Drain() []identity.Identity {
1176		w.mu.Lock()
1177		defer w.mu.Unlock()
1178		ids := w.Changes()
1179		w.clear()
1180		return ids
1181	}

1193	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsFilter(events []fsnotify.Event) []fsnotify.Event {
1194		seen := make(map[fsnotify.Event]bool)
1195	
1196		n := 0
1197		for _, ev := range events {
1198			// Avoid processing the same event twice.
1199			if seen[ev] {
1200				continue
1201			}
1202			seen[ev] = true
1203	
1204			if h.SourceSpec.IgnoreFile(ev.Name) {
1205				continue
1206			}
1207	
1208			if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" { // When a file system is HFS+, its filepath is in NFD form.
1209				ev.Name = norm.NFC.String(ev.Name)
1210			}
1211	
1212			events[n] = ev
1213			n++
1214		}
1215		events = events[:n]
1216	
1217		eventOrdinal := func(e fsnotify.Event) int {
1218			// Pull the structural changes to the top.
1219			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
1220				return 1
1221			}
1222			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Remove) {
1223				return 2
1224			}
1225			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Rename) {
1226				return 3
1227			}
1228			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
1229				return 4
1230			}
1231			return 5
1232		}
1233	
1234		sort.Slice(events, func(i, j int) bool {
1235			// First sort by event type.
1236			if eventOrdinal(events[i]) != eventOrdinal(events[j]) {
1237				return eventOrdinal(events[i]) < eventOrdinal(events[j])
1238			}
1239			// Then sort by name.
1240			return events[i].Name < events[j].Name
1241		})
1242	
1243		return events
1244	}
1245	
1246	type fileEventInfo struct {
1247		fsnotify.Event
1248		fi           os.FileInfo
1249		added        bool
1250		removed      bool
1251		isChangedDir bool
1252	}
1253	
1254	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsApplyInfo(events []fsnotify.Event) []fileEventInfo {
1255		var infos []fileEventInfo
1256		for _, ev := range events {
1257			removed := false
1258			added := false
1259	
1260			if ev.Op&fsnotify.Remove == fsnotify.Remove {
1261				removed = true
1262			}
1263	
1264			fi, statErr := h.Fs.Source.Stat(ev.Name)
1265	
1266			// Some editors (Vim) sometimes issue only a Rename operation when writing an existing file
1267			// Sometimes a rename operation means that file has been renamed other times it means
1268			// it's been updated.
1269			if ev.Op.Has(fsnotify.Rename) {
1270				// If the file is still on disk, it's only been updated, if it's not, it's been moved
1271				if statErr != nil {
1272					removed = true
1273				}
1274			}
1275			if ev.Op.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
1276				added = true
1277			}
1278	
1279			isChangedDir := statErr == nil && fi.IsDir()
1280	
1281			infos = append(infos, fileEventInfo{
1282				Event:        ev,
1283				fi:           fi,
1284				added:        added,
1285				removed:      removed,
1286				isChangedDir: isChangedDir,
1287			})
1288		}
1289	
1290		n := 0
1291	
1292		for _, ev := range infos {
1293			// Remove any directories that's also represented by a file.
1294			keep := true
1295			if ev.isChangedDir {
1296				for _, ev2 := range infos {
1297					if ev2.fi != nil && !ev2.fi.IsDir() && filepath.Dir(ev2.Name) == ev.Name {
1298						keep = false
1299						break
1300					}
1301				}
1302			}
1303			if keep {
1304				infos[n] = ev
1305				n++
1306			}
1307		}
1308		infos = infos[:n]
1309	
1310		return infos
1311	}
1312	
1313	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsTrim(events []fsnotify.Event) []fsnotify.Event {
1314		seen := make(map[string]bool)
1315		n := 0
1316		for _, ev := range events {
1317			if seen[ev.Name] {
1318				continue
1319			}
1320			seen[ev.Name] = true
1321			events[n] = ev
1322			n++
1323		}
1324		return events
1325	}
1326	
1327	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsContentPaths(p []pathChange) []pathChange {
1328		var bundles []pathChange
1329		var dirs []pathChange
1330		var regular []pathChange
1331	
1332		var others []pathChange
1333		for _, p := range p {
1334			if p.isDir {
1335				dirs = append(dirs, p)
1336			} else {
1337				others = append(others, p)
1338			}
1339		}
1340	
1341		// Remove all files below dir.
1342		if len(dirs) > 0 {
1343			n := 0
1344			for _, o := range others {
1345				keep := true
1346				for _, d := range dirs {
1347					if strings.HasPrefix(o.p.Path(), d.p.Path()+"/") {
1348						keep = false
1349						break
1350					}
1351				}
1352				if keep {
1353					others[n] = o
1354					n++
1355				}
1356			}
1357			others = others[:n]
1358		}
1359	
1360		for _, p := range others {
1361			if p.p.IsBundle() {
1362				bundles = append(bundles, p)
1363			} else {
1364				regular = append(regular, p)
1365			}
1366		}
1367	
1368		// Remove any files below leaf bundles.
1369		// Remove any files in the same folder as branch bundles.
1370		var keepers []pathChange
1371	
1372		for _, o := range regular {
1373			keep := true
1374			for _, b := range bundles {
1375				prefix := b.p.Base() + "/"
1376				if b.p.IsLeafBundle() && strings.HasPrefix(o.p.Path(), prefix) {
1377					keep = false
1378					break
1379				} else if b.p.IsBranchBundle() && o.p.Dir() == b.p.Dir() {
1380					keep = false
1381					break
1382				}
1383			}
1384	
1385			if keep {
1386				keepers = append(keepers, o)
1387			}
1388		}
1389	
1390		keepers = append(dirs, keepers...)
1391		keepers = append(bundles, keepers...)
1392	
1393		return keepers
1394	}
1395	
1396	// SitemapAbsURL is a convenience method giving the absolute URL to the sitemap.
1397	func (s *Site) SitemapAbsURL() string {
1398		base := ""
1399		if s.Conf.IsMultilingual() || s.Conf.DefaultContentLanguageInSubdir() {
1400			base = s.Language().Lang
1401		}
1402		p := s.AbsURL(base, false)
1403		if !strings.HasSuffix(p, "/") {
1404			p += "/"
1405		}
1406		p += s.conf.Sitemap.Filename
1407		return p
1408	}
1409	
1410	func (s *Site) createNodeMenuEntryURL(in string) string {
1411		if !strings.HasPrefix(in, "/") {
1412			return in
1413		}
1414		// make it match the nodes
1415		menuEntryURL := in
1416		menuEntryURL = s.PathSpec.URLize(menuEntryURL)
1417		if !s.conf.CanonifyURLs {
1418			menuEntryURL = paths.AddContextRoot(s.PathSpec.Cfg.BaseURL().String(), menuEntryURL)
1419		}
1420		return menuEntryURL
1421	}
1422	
1423	func (s *Site) assembleMenus() (navigation.Menus, error) {
1424		menus := make(navigation.Menus)
1425	
1426		type twoD struct {
1427			MenuName, EntryName string
1428		}
1429		flat := map[twoD]*navigation.MenuEntry{}
1430		children := map[twoD]navigation.Menu{}
1431	
1432		// add menu entries from config to flat hash
1433		for name, menu := range s.conf.Menus.Config {
1434			for _, x := range menu {
1435				// Shallow copy the menu entry, so we can set the URL and page without modifying the config.
1436				c := *x
1437				me := &c
1438	
1439				if me.PageRef != "" {
1440					// Try to resolve the page.
1441					me.Page, _ = s.getPage(nil, me.PageRef)
1442				}
1443	
1444				// If page is still nill, we must make sure that we have a URL that considers baseURL etc.
1445				if types.IsNil(me.Page) {
1446					me.ConfiguredURL = s.createNodeMenuEntryURL(me.MenuConfig.URL)
1447				} else {
1448					navigation.SetPageValues(me, me.Page)
1449				}
1450	
1451				flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}] = me
1452			}
1453		}
1454	
1455		sectionPagesMenu := s.conf.SectionPagesMenu
1456	
1457		if sectionPagesMenu != "" {
1458			if err := s.pageMap.forEachPage(pagePredicates.ShouldListGlobal, func(p *pageState) (bool, error) {
1459				if p.Kind() != kinds.KindSection || !p.m.shouldBeCheckedForMenuDefinitions() {
1460					return false, nil
1461				}
1462	
1463				// The section pages menus are attached to the top level section.
1464				id := p.Section()
1465				if id == "" {
1466					id = "/"
1467				}
1468	
1469				if _, ok := flat[twoD{sectionPagesMenu, id}]; ok {
1470					return false, nil
1471				}
1472				me := navigation.MenuEntry{
1473					MenuConfig: navigation.MenuConfig{
1474						Identifier: id,
1475						Name:       p.LinkTitle(),
1476						Weight:     p.Weight(),
1477					},
1478					Page: p,
1479				}
1480	
1481				navigation.SetPageValues(&me, p)
1482				flat[twoD{sectionPagesMenu, me.KeyName()}] = &me
1483				return false, nil
1484			}); err != nil {
1485				return nil, err
1486			}
1487		}
1488	
1489		// Add menu entries provided by pages
1490		if err := s.pageMap.forEachPage(pagePredicates.ShouldListGlobal, func(p *pageState) (bool, error) {
1491			for name, me := range p.pageMenus.menus() {
1492				if _, ok := flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}]; ok {
1493					err := p.wrapError(fmt.Errorf("duplicate menu entry with identifier %q in menu %q", me.KeyName(), name))
1494					s.Log.Warnln(err)
1495					continue
1496				}
1497				flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}] = me
1498			}
1499			return false, nil
1500		}); err != nil {
1501			return nil, err
1502		}
1503	
1504		// Create Children Menus First
1505		for _, e := range flat {
1506			if e.Parent != "" {
1507				children[twoD{e.Menu, e.Parent}] = children[twoD{e.Menu, e.Parent}].Add(e)
1508			}
1509		}
1510	
1511		// Placing Children in Parents (in flat)
1512		for p, childmenu := range children {
1513			_, ok := flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}]
1514			if !ok {
1515				// if parent does not exist, create one without a URL
1516				flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}] = &navigation.MenuEntry{
1517					MenuConfig: navigation.MenuConfig{
1518						Name: p.EntryName,
1519					},
1520				}
1521			}
1522			flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}].Children = childmenu
1523		}
1524	
1525		// Assembling Top Level of Tree
1526		for menu, e := range flat {
1527			if e.Parent == "" {
1528				_, ok := menus[menu.MenuName]
1529				if !ok {
1530					menus[menu.MenuName] = navigation.Menu{}
1531				}
1532				menus[menu.MenuName] = menus[menu.MenuName].Add(e)
1533			}
1534		}
1535	
1536		return menus, nil
1537	}
1538	
1539	// get any language code to prefix the target file path with.
1540	func (s *Site) getLanguageTargetPathLang(alwaysInSubDir bool) string {
1541		if s.h.Conf.IsMultihost() {
1542			return s.Language().Lang
1543		}
1544	
1545		return s.getLanguagePermalinkLang(alwaysInSubDir)
1546	}
1547	
1548	// get any language code to prefix the relative permalink with.
1549	func (s *Site) getLanguagePermalinkLang(alwaysInSubDir bool) string {
1550		if s.h.Conf.IsMultihost() {
1551			return ""
1552		}
1553	
1554		if s.h.Conf.IsMultilingual() && alwaysInSubDir {
1555			return s.Language().Lang
1556		}
1557	
1558		return s.GetLanguagePrefix()
1559	}
1560	
1561	func (s *Site) getPrefixRole() string {
1562		role := s.roleInternal
1563		if role.Default {
1564			if s.conf.DefaultContentRoleInSubdir {
1565				return role.Name
1566			}
1567			return ""
1568		}
1569		return role.Name
1570	}
1571	
1572	func (s *Site) getPrefixVersion() string {
1573		version := s.versionInternal
1574		if version.Default {
1575			if s.conf.DefaultContentVersionInSubdir {
1576				return version.Name
1577			}
1578			return ""
1579		}
1580		return version.Name
1581	}
1582	
1583	// Prepare site for a new full build.
1584	func (s *Site) resetBuildState(sourceChanged bool) {
1585		s.relatedDocsHandler = s.relatedDocsHandler.Clone()
1586		s.init.Reset()
1587		s.pageMap.Reset()
1588	}
1589	
1590	func (s *Site) errorCollator(results <-chan error, errs chan<- error) {
1591		var errors []error
1592		defer func() {
1593			errs <- s.h.filterAndJoinErrors(errors)
1594			close(errs)
1595		}()
1596		const maxErrors = 10
1597		for e := range results {
1598			errors = append(errors, e)
1599			if len(errors) >= maxErrors {
1600				s.h.Stop()
1601				break
1602			}
1603		}
1604	}
1605	
1606	// GetPage looks up a page of a given type for the given ref.
1607	// In Hugo <= 0.44 you had to add Page Kind (section, home) etc. as the first
1608	// argument and then either a unix styled path (with or without a leading slash))
1609	// or path elements separated.
1610	// When we now remove the Kind from this API, we need to make the transition as painless
1611	// as possible for existing sites. Most sites will use {{ .Site.GetPage "section" "my/section" }},
1612	// i.e. 2 arguments, so we test for that.
1613	func (s *Site) GetPage(ref ...string) (page.Page, error) {
1614		s.CheckReady()
1615		p, err := s.getPageForRefs(ref...)
1616	
1617		if p == nil {
1618			// The nil struct has meaning in some situations, mostly to avoid breaking
1619			// existing sites doing $nilpage.IsDescendant($p), which will always return
1620			// false.
1621			p = page.NilPage
1622		}
1623	
1624		return p, err
1625	}
1626	
1627	func (s *Site) absURLPath(targetPath string) string {
1628		var path string
1629		if s.conf.RelativeURLs {
1630			path = helpers.GetDottedRelativePath(targetPath)
1631		} else {
1632			url := s.PathSpec.Cfg.BaseURL().String()
1633			if !strings.HasSuffix(url, "/") {
1634				url += "/"
1635			}
1636			path = url
1637		}
1638	
1639		return path
1640	}
1641	
1642	const (
1643		pageDependencyScopeDefault int = iota
1644		pageDependencyScopeGlobal
1645	)
1646	
1647	func (s *Site) renderAndWritePage(statCounter *uint64, targetPath string, p *pageState, d any, templ *tplimpl.TemplInfo) error {
1648		s.h.onPageRender()
1649		renderBuffer := bp.GetBuffer()
1650		defer bp.PutBuffer(renderBuffer)
1651	
1652		of := p.outputFormat()
1653		p.incrRenderState()
1654	
1655		ctx := s.TemplateStore.PrepareTopLevelRenderCtx(context.Background(), p)
1656		ctx = tpl.Context.DependencyManagerScopedProvider.Set(ctx, p)
1657	
1658		if err := s.renderForTemplate(ctx, p.Kind(), of.Name, d, renderBuffer, templ); err != nil {
1659			return err
1660		}
1661	
1662		if renderBuffer.Len() == 0 {
1663			return nil
1664		}
1665	
1666		isHTML := of.IsHTML
1667		isRSS := of.Name == "rss"
1668	
1669		pd := publisher.Descriptor{
1670			Src:          renderBuffer,
1671			TargetPath:   targetPath,
1672			StatCounter:  statCounter,
1673			OutputFormat: p.outputFormat(),
1674		}
1675	
1676		if isRSS {
1677			// Always canonify URLs in RSS
1678			pd.AbsURLPath = s.absURLPath(targetPath)
1679		} else if isHTML {
1680			if s.conf.RelativeURLs || s.conf.CanonifyURLs {
1681				pd.AbsURLPath = s.absURLPath(targetPath)
1682			}
1683	
1684			if s.watching() && s.conf.Internal.Running && !s.conf.DisableLiveReload {
1685				pd.LiveReloadBaseURL = s.Conf.BaseURLLiveReload().URL()
1686			}
1687	
1688			// For performance reasons we only inject the Hugo generator tag on the home page.
1689			if p.IsHome() {
1690				pd.AddHugoGeneratorTag = !s.conf.DisableHugoGeneratorInject
1691			}
1692	
1693		}
1694	
1695		return s.publisher.Publish(pd)
1696	}
1697	
1698	var infoOnMissingLayout = map[string]bool{
1699		// The 404 layout is very much optional in Hugo, but we do look for it.
1700		"404": true,
1701	}
1702	
1703	// hookRendererTemplate is the canonical implementation of all hooks.ITEMRenderer,
1704	// where ITEM is the thing being hooked.
1705	type hookRendererTemplate struct {
1706		templateHandler *tplimpl.TemplateStore
1707		templ           *tplimpl.TemplInfo
1708		resolvePosition func(renderContext any, pos int) text.Position
1709	}
1710	
1711	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderLink(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.LinkContext) error {
1712		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1713	}
1714	
1715	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderHeading(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.HeadingContext) error {
1716		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1717	}
1718	
1719	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderCodeblock(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.CodeblockContext) error {
1720		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1721	}
1722	
1723	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderPassthrough(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.PassthroughContext) error {
1724		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1725	}
1726	
1727	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderBlockquote(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.BlockquoteContext) error {
1728		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1729	}
1730	
1731	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderTable(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.TableContext) error {
1732		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1733	}
1734	
1735	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) ResolvePosition(renderContext any, pos int) text.Position {
1736		return hr.resolvePosition(renderContext, pos)
1737	}
1738	
1739	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) IsDefaultCodeBlockRenderer() bool {
1740		return false
1741	}
1742	
1743	func (s *Site) renderForTemplate(ctx context.Context, name, outputFormat string, d any, w io.Writer, templ *tplimpl.TemplInfo) (err error) {
1744		if templ == nil {
1745			s.logMissingLayout(name, "", "", outputFormat)
1746			return nil
1747		}
1748	
1749		if ctx == nil {
1750			panic("nil context")
1751		}
1752	
1753		if err = s.GetTemplateStore().ExecuteWithContext(ctx, templ, w, d); err != nil {
1754			filename := name
1755			if p, ok := d.(*pageState); ok {
1756				filename = p.String()
1757			}
1758			return fmt.Errorf("render of %q failed: %w", filename, err)
1759		}
1760		return
1761	}
1762	
1763	func (s *Site) shouldBuild(p page.Page) bool {
1764		if !s.conf.IsKindEnabled(p.Kind()) {
1765			return false
1766		}
1767		return shouldBuild(s.Conf.BuildFuture(), s.Conf.BuildExpired(),
1768			s.Conf.BuildDrafts(), p.Draft(), p.PublishDate(), p.ExpiryDate())
1769	}
1770	
1771	func shouldBuild(buildFuture bool, buildExpired bool, buildDrafts bool, Draft bool,
1772		publishDate time.Time, expiryDate time.Time,
1773	) bool {
1774		if !(buildDrafts || !Draft) {
1775			return false
1776		}
1777		hnow := htime.Now()
1778		if !buildFuture && !publishDate.IsZero() && publishDate.After(hnow) {
1779			return false
1780		}
1781		if !buildExpired && !expiryDate.IsZero() && expiryDate.Before(hnow) {
1782			return false
1783		}
1784		return true
1785	}
1786	
1787	func (s *Site) render(ctx *siteRenderContext) (err error) {
1788		if err := page.Clear(); err != nil {
1789			return err
1790		}
1791	
1792		if err = s.renderPages(ctx); err != nil {
1793			return
1794		}
1795	
1796		if !ctx.shouldRenderStandalonePage("") {
1797			return
1798		}
1799	
1800		return
1801	}
1802	
1803	func (s *Site) String() string {
1804		if s == nil {
1805			return "Site (nil)"
1806		}
1807	
1808		return fmt.Sprintf("Site %v: %s", s.siteVector, s.resolveDimensionNames())
1809	}
1810

13	
14	package publisher
15	
16	import (
17		"errors"
18		"fmt"
19		"io"
20		"net/url"
21		"sync/atomic"
22	
23		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources"
24	
25		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/media"
26	
27		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/minifiers"
28	
29		bp "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/bufferpool"
30		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/helpers"
31	
32		"github.com/spf13/afero"
33	
34		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/output"
35		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/transform"
36		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/transform/livereloadinject"
37		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/transform/metainject"
38		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/transform/urlreplacers"
39	)
40	
41	// Descriptor describes the needed publishing chain for an item.
42	type Descriptor struct {
43		// The content to publish.
44		Src io.Reader
45	
46		// The OutputFormat of the this content.
47		OutputFormat output.Format
48	
49		// Where to publish this content. This is a filesystem-relative path.
50		TargetPath string
51	
52		// Counter for the end build summary.
53		StatCounter *uint64
54	
55		// Configuration that trigger pre-processing.
56		// LiveReload script will be injected if this is != nil
57		LiveReloadBaseURL *url.URL
58	
59		// Enable to inject the Hugo generated tag in the header. Is currently only
60		// injected on the home page for HTML type of output formats.
61		AddHugoGeneratorTag bool
62	
63		// If set, will replace all relative URLs with this one.
64		AbsURLPath string
65	
66		// Enable to minify the output using the OutputFormat defined above to
67		// pick the correct minifier configuration.
68		Minify bool
69	}
70	
71	// DestinationPublisher is the default and currently only publisher in Hugo. This
72	// publisher prepares and publishes an item to the defined destination, e.g. /public.
73	type DestinationPublisher struct {
74		fs                    afero.Fs
75		min                   minifiers.Client
76		htmlElementsCollector *htmlElementsCollector
77	}
78	
79	// NewDestinationPublisher creates a new DestinationPublisher.
80	func NewDestinationPublisher(rs *resources.Spec, outputFormats output.Formats, mediaTypes media.Types) (pub DestinationPublisher, err error) {
81		fs := rs.BaseFs.PublishFs
82		cfg := rs.Cfg
83		var classCollector *htmlElementsCollector
84		if rs.BuildConfig().BuildStats.Enabled() {
85			classCollector = newHTMLElementsCollector(rs.BuildConfig().BuildStats)
86		}
87		pub = DestinationPublisher{fs: fs, htmlElementsCollector: classCollector}
88		pub.min, err = minifiers.New(mediaTypes, outputFormats, cfg)
89		return
90	}
91	
92	// Publish applies any relevant transformations and writes the file
93	// to its destination, e.g. /public.
94	func (p DestinationPublisher) Publish(d Descriptor) error {
95		if d.TargetPath == "" {
96			return errors.New("Publish: must provide a TargetPath")
97		}
98	
99		src := d.Src
100	
101		transformers := p.createTransformerChain(d)
102	
103		if len(transformers) != 0 {
104			b := bp.GetBuffer()
105			defer bp.PutBuffer(b)
106	
107			if err := transformers.Apply(b, d.Src); err != nil {
108				return fmt.Errorf("failed to process %q: %w", d.TargetPath, err)
109			}
110	
111			// This is now what we write to disk.
112			src = b
113		}
114	
115		f, err := helpers.OpenFileForWriting(p.fs, d.TargetPath)
116		if err != nil {
117			return err
118		}
119		defer f.Close()
120	
121		var w io.Writer = f
122	
123		if p.htmlElementsCollector != nil && d.OutputFormat.IsHTML {
124			w = io.MultiWriter(w, newHTMLElementsCollectorWriter(p.htmlElementsCollector))
125		}
126	
127		_, err = io.Copy(w, src)
128		if err == nil && d.StatCounter != nil {
129			atomic.AddUint64(d.StatCounter, uint64(1))
130		}
131	
132		return err
133	}
134	
135	func (p DestinationPublisher) PublishStats() PublishStats {
136		if p.htmlElementsCollector == nil {
137			return PublishStats{}
138		}
139	
140		return PublishStats{
141			HTMLElements: p.htmlElementsCollector.getHTMLElements(),
142		}
143	}
144	
145	type PublishStats struct {
146		HTMLElements HTMLElements `json:"htmlElements"`
147	}
148	
149	// Publisher publishes a result file.
150	type Publisher interface {
151		Publish(d Descriptor) error
152		PublishStats() PublishStats
153	}
154	
155	// XML transformer := transform.New(urlreplacers.NewAbsURLInXMLTransformer(path))
156	func (p DestinationPublisher) createTransformerChain(f Descriptor) transform.Chain {
157		transformers := transform.NewEmpty()
158	
159		isHTML := f.OutputFormat.IsHTML
160	
161		if f.AbsURLPath != "" {
162			if isHTML {
163				transformers = append(transformers, urlreplacers.NewAbsURLTransformer(f.AbsURLPath))
164			} else {
165				// Assume XML.
166				transformers = append(transformers, urlreplacers.NewAbsURLInXMLTransformer(f.AbsURLPath))
167			}
168		}
169	
170		if isHTML {
171			if f.LiveReloadBaseURL != nil {
172				transformers = append(transformers, livereloadinject.New(f.LiveReloadBaseURL))
173			}
174	
175			// This is only injected on the home page.
176			if f.AddHugoGeneratorTag {
177				transformers = append(transformers, metainject.HugoGenerator)
178			}
179	
180		}
181	
182		if p.min.MinifyOutput {
183			minifyTransformer := p.min.Transformer(f.OutputFormat.MediaType)
184			if minifyTransformer != nil {
185				transformers = append(transformers, minifyTransformer)
186			}
187		}
188	
189		return transformers
190	}
191

26	
27	func newDeployCommand() simplecobra.Commander {
28		return &simpleCommand{
29			name:  "deploy",
30			short: "Deploy your project to a cloud provider",
31			long: `Deploy your project to a cloud provider
32	
33	See https://gohugo.io/hosting-and-deployment/hugo-deploy/ for detailed
34	documentation.
35	`,
36			run: func(ctx context.Context, cd *simplecobra.Commandeer, r *rootCommand, args []string) error {
37				h, err := r.Hugo(flagsToCfgWithAdditionalConfigBase(cd, nil, "deployment"))
38				if err != nil {
39					return err
40				}
41				deployer, err := deploy.New(h.Configs.GetFirstLanguageConfig(), h.Log, h.PathSpec.PublishFs)
42				if err != nil {
43					return err
44				}
45				return deployer.Deploy(ctx)
46			},
47			withc: func(cmd *cobra.Command, r *rootCommand) {
48				applyDeployFlags(cmd, r)
49			},
50		}
51	}
52

122	func (s Site) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) (*Site, error) {

198	// NewHugoSites creates HugoSites from the given config.
199	func NewHugoSites(cfg deps.DepsCfg) (*HugoSites, error) {
200		if !cfg.TestCfg.IsZero() && !cfg.IsIntegrationTest {
201			panic("DepsCfg.TestCfg must only be set in integration tests")
202		}
203	
204		conf := cfg.Configs.GetFirstLanguageConfig()
205		rolesSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Roles.Config.Sorted
206		versionsSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Versions.Config.Sorted
207	
208		var (
209			poolSizeKatex = 2
210			poolSizeWebP  = 1
211			poolSizeAvif  = 1
212		)
213		if n := config.GetNumWorkerMultiplier(); n > 1 {
214			poolSizeKatex = min(n, 8)
215			poolSizeWebP = max(2, n/2)
216			poolSizeAvif = max(2, n/2)
217		}
218	
219		var logger loggers.Logger
220		if cfg.TestLogger != nil {
221			logger = cfg.TestLogger
222		} else {
223			var logHookLast func(e *logg.Entry) error
224			if cfg.Configs.Base.PanicOnWarning {
225				logHookLast = loggers.PanicOnWarningHook
226			}
227			if cfg.StdOut == nil {
228				cfg.StdOut = os.Stdout
229			}
230			if cfg.StdErr == nil {
231				cfg.StdErr = os.Stderr
232			}
233			if cfg.LogLevel == 0 {
234				cfg.LogLevel = logg.LevelWarn
235			}
236	
237			logOpts := loggers.Options{
238				Level:              cfg.LogLevel,
239				DistinctLevel:      logg.LevelWarn, // This will drop duplicate log warning and errors.
240				HandlerPost:        logHookLast,
241				StdOut:             cfg.StdOut,
242				StdErr:             cfg.StdErr,
243				StoreErrors:        conf.Watching(),
244				SuppressStatements: conf.IgnoredLogs(),
245			}
246			logger = loggers.New(logOpts)
247	
248		}
249	
250		memCache := dynacache.New(dynacache.Options{Watching: conf.Watching(), Log: logger})
251	
252		var h *HugoSites
253		onSignalRebuild := func(ids ...identity.Identity) {
254			// This channel is buffered, but make sure we do this in a non-blocking way.
255			if cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
256				go func() {
257					cfg.ChangesFromBuild <- ids
258				}()
259			}
260		}
261	
262		compilationCacheDir := filepath.Join(conf.Dirs().CacheDir, "_warpc")
263	
264		imageWasmMemory := 384 // 384 MiB (4096 MiB Max)
265		if m := cfg.TestCfg.WarpcMemory; m > 0 {
266			imageWasmMemory = m
267		}
268	
269		firstSiteDeps := &deps.Deps{
270			Fs:   cfg.Fs,
271			Log:  logger,
272			Conf: conf,
273			BuildState: &deps.BuildState{
274				OnSignalRebuild: onSignalRebuild,
275			},
276			Counters:            &deps.Counters{},
277			MemCache:            memCache,
278			TranslationProvider: i18n.NewTranslationProvider(),
279			WasmDispatchers: warpc.AllDispatchers(
280				// Katex options.
281				warpc.Options{
282					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
283	
284					PoolSize: poolSizeKatex,
285					Infof:    logger.InfoCommand("katex").Logf,
286					Warnf:    logger.WarnCommand("katex").Logf,
287				},
288				// WebP options.
289				warpc.Options{
290					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
291					PoolSize:            poolSizeWebP,
292					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
293					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("webp").Logf,
294					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("webp").Logf,
295				},
296				// Avif options.
297				warpc.Options{
298					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
299					PoolSize:            poolSizeAvif,
300					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
301					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("avif").Logf,
302					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("avif").Logf,
303				},
304			),
305		}
306	
307		if err := firstSiteDeps.Init(); err != nil {
308			return nil, err
309		}
310	
311		// Prevent leaking goroutines in tests.
312		if cfg.IsIntegrationTest && cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
313			firstSiteDeps.BuildClosers.Add(types.CloserFunc(func() error {
314				close(cfg.ChangesFromBuild)
315				return nil
316			}))
317		}
318	
319		batcherClient, err := esbuild.NewBatcherClient(firstSiteDeps)
320		if err != nil {
321			return nil, err
322		}
323		firstSiteDeps.JSBatcherClient = batcherClient
324	
325		confm := cfg.Configs
326		if err := confm.Validate(logger); err != nil {
327			return nil, err
328		}
329		var sites []*Site
330	
331		ns := &contentNodeShifter{
332			conf: conf,
333		}
334	
335		treeConfig := doctree.Config[contentNode]{
336			Shifter:        ns,
337			TransformerRaw: &contentNodeTransformerRaw{},
338		}
339	
340		dimensionLengths := sitesmatrix.Vector{len(confm.Languages), len(versionsSorted), len(rolesSorted)}
341	
342		pageTrees := &pageTrees{
343			treePages: doctree.New(
344				treeConfig,
345			),
346			treeResources: doctree.New(
347				treeConfig,
348			),
349			treeTaxonomyEntries:           doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*weightedContentNode](dimensionLengths),
350			treePagesFromTemplateAdapters: doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*pagesfromdata.PagesFromTemplate](dimensionLengths),
351		}
352	
353		pageTrees.createMutableTrees()
354	
355		for i, confp := range confm.ConfigLangs() {
356			language := confp.Language().(*langs.Language)
357			if language.Disabled {
358				panic("cannot create site for disabled language: " + language.Lang)
359			}
360			k := language.Lang
361			conf := confm.LanguageConfigMap[k]
362			frontmatterHandler, err := pagemeta.NewFrontmatterHandler(firstSiteDeps.Log, conf.Frontmatter)
363			if err != nil {
364				return nil, err
365			}
366	
367			langs.SetParams(language, conf.Params)
368	
369			s := &Site{
370				conf:               conf,
371				language:           language,
372				frontmatterHandler: frontmatterHandler,
373				store:              hstore.NewScratch(),
374				siteLanguageVersionRole: &siteLanguageVersionRole{
375					isDefaultLanguage: language.IsDefault(),
376					siteVector:        sitesmatrix.Vector{i, 0, 0},
377				},
378			}
379	
380			s.siteWrapped = page.WrapSite(s)
381	
382			if i == 0 {
383				firstSiteDeps.Site = s
384				s.Deps = firstSiteDeps
385			} else {
386				d, err := firstSiteDeps.Clone(s, confp)
387				if err != nil {
388					return nil, err
389				}
390				s.Deps = d
391			}
392	
393			// Set up the main publishing chain.
394			pub, err := publisher.NewDestinationPublisher(
395				firstSiteDeps.ResourceSpec,
396				s.conf.OutputFormats.Config,
397				s.conf.MediaTypes.Config,
398			)
399			if err != nil {

56	type hugoBuilder struct {
57		r *rootCommand
58	
59		confmu  sync.Mutex
60		confOld *commonConfig
61		conf    *commonConfig
62	
63		// May be nil.
64		s *serverCommand
65	
66		// Currently only set when in "fast render mode".
67		changeDetector *fileChangeDetector
68		visitedURLs    *types.EvictingQueue[string]
69	
70		fullRebuildSem *semaphore.Weighted
71		debounce       func(f func())
72	
73		onConfigLoaded func(reloaded bool) error
74	
75		fastRenderMode     bool
76		showErrorInBrowser bool
77	
78		errState hugoBuilderErrState
79	}
80	
81	var errConfigNotSet = errors.New("config not set")
82	
83	func (c *hugoBuilder) withConfE(fn func(conf *commonConfig) error) error {
84		c.confmu.Lock()
85		defer c.confmu.Unlock()
86		if c.conf == nil {
87			return errConfigNotSet
88		}
89		return fn(c.conf)
90	}
91	
92	func (c *hugoBuilder) withConf(fn func(conf *commonConfig)) {
93		c.confmu.Lock()
94		defer c.confmu.Unlock()
95		fn(c.conf)
96	}
97	
98	func (c *hugoBuilder) withConfOrOldConf(fn func(conf *commonConfig)) {
99		c.confmu.Lock()
100		defer c.confmu.Unlock()
101		if c.conf != nil {
102			fn(c.conf)
103		} else if c.confOld != nil {
104			fn(c.confOld)
105		}
106	}
107	
108	func (c *hugoBuilder) withConfOrOldConfE(fn func(conf *commonConfig) error) error {
109		c.confmu.Lock()
110		defer c.confmu.Unlock()
111		if c.conf != nil {
112			return fn(c.conf)
113		} else if c.confOld != nil {
114			return fn(c.confOld)
115		}
116		return errConfigNotSet
117	}
118	
119	type hugoBuilderErrState struct {
120		mu       sync.Mutex
121		paused   bool
122		builderr error
123		waserr   bool
124	}
125	
126	func (e *hugoBuilderErrState) setPaused(p bool) {
127		e.mu.Lock()
128		defer e.mu.Unlock()
129		e.paused = p
130	}
131	
132	func (e *hugoBuilderErrState) isPaused() bool {
133		e.mu.Lock()
134		defer e.mu.Unlock()
135		return e.paused
136	}
137	
138	func (e *hugoBuilderErrState) setBuildErr(err error) {
139		e.mu.Lock()
140		defer e.mu.Unlock()
141		e.builderr = err
142	}
143	
144	func (e *hugoBuilderErrState) buildErr() error {
145		e.mu.Lock()
146		defer e.mu.Unlock()
147		return e.builderr
148	}
149	
150	func (e *hugoBuilderErrState) setWasErr(w bool) {
151		e.mu.Lock()
152		defer e.mu.Unlock()
153		e.waserr = w
154	}
155	
156	func (e *hugoBuilderErrState) wasErr() bool {
157		e.mu.Lock()
158		defer e.mu.Unlock()
159		return e.waserr
160	}
161	
162	// getDirList provides NewWatcher() with a list of directories to watch for changes.
163	func (c *hugoBuilder) getDirList() ([]string, error) {
164		h, err := c.hugo()
165		if err != nil {
166			return nil, err
167		}
168	
169		return hstrings.UniqueStringsSorted(h.PathSpec.BaseFs.WatchFilenames()), nil
170	}
171	
172	func (c *hugoBuilder) initCPUProfile() (func(), error) {
173		if c.r.cpuprofile == "" {
174			return nil, nil
175		}
176	
177		f, err := os.Create(c.r.cpuprofile)
178		if err != nil {
179			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create CPU profile: %w", err)
180		}
181		if err := pprof.StartCPUProfile(f); err != nil {
182			f.Close()
183			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start CPU profile: %w", err)
184		}
185		return func() {
186			pprof.StopCPUProfile()
187			f.Close()
188		}, nil
189	}
190	
191	func (c *hugoBuilder) initMemProfile() {
192		if c.r.memprofile == "" {
193			return
194		}
195	
196		f, err := os.Create(c.r.memprofile)
197		if err != nil {
198			c.r.logger.Errorf("could not create memory profile: ", err)
199		}
200		defer f.Close()
