45		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/images"
46		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/page"
47		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/resource"
48	)
49	
50	func NewSpec(
51		s *helpers.PathSpec,
52		common *SpecCommon, // may be nil
53		wasmDispatchers *warpc.Dispatchers,
54		fileCaches filecache.Caches,
55		memCache *dynacache.Cache,
56		incr identity.Incrementer,
57		logger loggers.Logger,
58		errorHandler herrors.ErrorSender,
59		execHelper *hexec.Exec,
60		buildClosers types.CloseAdder,
61		rebuilder Rebuilder,
62	) (*Spec, error) {
63		conf := s.Cfg.GetConfig().(*allconfig.Config)
64		imgConfig := conf.Imaging
65	
66		imagesDebugl := logger.DebugCommand("images")
67		imagesWarnl := logger.WarnCommand("images")
68	
69		imaging, err := images.NewImageProcessor(imagesDebugl, imagesWarnl, wasmDispatchers, imgConfig)
70		if err != nil {
71			return nil, err
72		}
73	
74		if incr == nil {
75			incr = &identity.IncrementByOne{}
76		}
77	
78		if logger == nil {
79			logger = loggers.NewDefault()
80		}
81	
82		permalinks, err := page.NewPermalinkExpander(s.URLize, conf.Permalinks)
83		if err != nil {
84			return nil, err
85		}
86	
87		if common == nil {
88			common = &SpecCommon{
89				incr:       incr,
90				FileCaches: fileCaches,
91				PostBuildAssets: &PostBuildAssets{
92					PostProcessResources: maphelpers.NewConcurrentMap[string, postpub.PostPublishedResource](),
93					JSConfigBuilder:      jsconfig.NewBuilder(),
94				},
95			}
96		}
97	
98		rs := &Spec{
99			PathSpec:     s,
100			Logger:       logger,
101			ErrorSender:  errorHandler,
102			BuildClosers: buildClosers,
103			Rebuilder:    rebuilder,
104			Imaging:      imaging,
105			ImageCache: newImageCache(
106				fileCaches.ImageCache(),
107				memCache,
108				s,
109			),
110			ExecHelper: execHelper,
111	
112			Permalinks: permalinks,
113	
114			SpecCommon: common,
115		}
116	
117		rs.ResourceCache = newResourceCache(rs, memCache)
118	
119		return rs, nil
120	}
121	
122	type Rebuilder interface {
123		identity.SignalRebuilder
124		identity.IsRebuildProvider
125	}
126	
127	type Spec struct {
128		*helpers.PathSpec
129	
130		Logger       loggers.Logger
131		ErrorSender  herrors.ErrorSender
132		BuildClosers types.CloseAdder
133		Rebuilder    Rebuilder
134	
135		Permalinks page.PermalinkExpander
136	
137		ImageCache *ImageCache
138	
139		// Holds default filter settings etc.
140		Imaging *images.ImageProcessor
141	
142		ExecHelper *hexec.Exec
143	
144		*SpecCommon
145	}
146	
147	// The parts of Spec that's common for all sites.
148	type SpecCommon struct {
149		incr          identity.Incrementer
150		ResourceCache *ResourceCache
151		FileCaches    filecache.Caches
152	
153		// Assets used after the build is done.
154		// This is shared between all sites.
155		*PostBuildAssets
156	}
157	
158	type PostBuildAssets struct {
159		PostProcessResources *maphelpers.ConcurrentMap[string, postpub.PostPublishedResource]
160		JSConfigBuilder      *jsconfig.Builder
161	}
162	
163	func (r *Spec) NewResourceWrapperFromResourceConfig(rc *pagemeta.ResourceConfig) (resource.Resource, error) {
164		content := rc.Content
165		switch r := content.Value.(type) {
166		case resource.Resource:
167			return cloneWithMetadataFromResourceConfigIfNeeded(rc, r), nil
168		default:
169			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create resource for path %q, expected a resource.Resource, got %T", rc.PathInfo.Path(), content.Value)
170		}
171	}
172	
173	// NewResource creates a new Resource from the given ResourceSourceDescriptor.
174	func (r *Spec) NewResource(rd ResourceSourceDescriptor) (resource.Resource, error) {
175		if err := rd.init(r); err != nil {
176			return nil, err
177		}
178	
179		dir, name := path.Split(rd.TargetPath)
180		dir = paths.ToSlashPreserveLeading(dir)
181		if dir == "/" {
182			dir = ""
183		}
184		rp := internal.ResourcePaths{
185			File:            name,
186			Dir:             dir,
187			BaseDirTarget:   rd.BasePathTargetPath,
188			BaseDirLink:     rd.BasePathRelPermalink,
189			TargetBasePaths: rd.TargetBasePaths,
190		}
191	
192		isImage := rd.MediaType.MainType == "image"
193		var imgFormat images.Format
194		var imgOpsSupport images.ImageResourceType
195		if isImage {
196			imgFormat, imgOpsSupport = images.ImageFormatFromMediaSubType(rd.MediaType.SubType)
197		}
198	
199		gr := &genericResource{
200			Staler:           &AtomicStaler{},
201			h:                &resourceHash{},
202			publishInit:      &hsync.OnceMore{},
203			keyInit:          &sync.Once{},
204			includeHashInKey: isImage,
205			paths:            rp,
206			spec:             r,
207			sd:               rd,
208			params:           rd.Params,
209			name:             rd.NameOriginal,
210			title:            rd.Title,
211		}
212	
213		if imgOpsSupport >= images.ImageResourceTypeMetaOnly {
214			ir := newImageResource(images.NewImage(imgFormat, r.Imaging, nil, gr), gr)
215			return newResourceAdapter(gr.spec, rd.LazyPublish, ir), nil
216		}
217	
218		return newResourceAdapter(gr.spec, rd.LazyPublish, gr), nil
219	}
220	
221	func (r *Spec) MediaTypes() media.Types {
222		return r.Cfg.GetConfigSection("mediaTypes").(media.Types)
223	}
224	
225	func (r *Spec) OutputFormats() output.Formats {
226		return r.Cfg.GetConfigSection("outputFormats").(output.Formats)
227	}
228	
229	func (r *Spec) BuildConfig() config.BuildConfig {
230		return r.Cfg.GetConfigSection("build").(config.BuildConfig)
231	}
232	
233	func (s *Spec) String() string {
234		return "spec"
235	}
236

55		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
56	)
57	
58	var errHelp = errors.New("help requested")
59	
60	// Execute executes a command.
61	func Execute(args []string) error {
62		// Default GOMAXPROCS to be CPU limit aware, still respecting GOMAXPROCS env.
63		maxprocs.Set()
64		x, err := newExec()
65		if err != nil {
66			return err
67		}
68		args = mapLegacyArgs(args)
69		cd, err := x.Execute(context.Background(), args)
70		if cd != nil {
71			if closer, ok := cd.Root.Command.(types.Closer); ok {
72				closer.Close()
73			}
74		}
75	
76		if err != nil {
77			if err == errHelp {
78				cd.CobraCommand.Help()
79				fmt.Println()
80				return nil
81			}
82			if simplecobra.IsCommandError(err) {
83				// Print the help, but also return the error to fail the command.
84				cd.CobraCommand.Help()
85				fmt.Println()
86			}
87		}
88		return err
89	}
90	
91	type commonConfig struct {
92		mu      *sync.Mutex
93		configs *allconfig.Configs
94		cfg     config.Provider
95		fs      *hugofs.Fs
96	}
97	
98	type configKey struct {
99		counter                    int32
100		ignoreModulesDoesNotExists bool
101		skipNpmCheck               bool
102	}
103	
104	// This is the root command.
105	type rootCommand struct {
106		Printf  func(format string, v ...any)
107		Println func(a ...any)
108		StdOut  io.Writer
109		StdErr  io.Writer
110	
111		logger loggers.Logger
112	
113		// The main cache busting key for the caches below.
114		configVersionID atomic.Int32
115	
116		// Some, but not all commands need access to these.
117		// Some needs more than one, so keep them in a small cache.
118		commonConfigs *lazycache.Cache[configKey, *commonConfig]
119		hugoSites     *lazycache.Cache[configKey, *hugolib.HugoSites]
120	
121		// changesFromBuild received from Hugo in watch mode.
122		changesFromBuild chan []identity.Identity
123	
124		commands []simplecobra.Commander
125	
126		// Flags
127		source      string
128		buildWatch  bool
129		environment string
130	
131		// Common build flags.
132		baseURL         string
133		gc              bool
134		poll            string
135		forceSyncStatic bool
136		panicOnWarning  bool
137	
138		// Profile flags (for debugging of performance problems)
139		cpuprofile   string
140		memprofile   string
141		mutexprofile string
142		traceprofile string
143		printm       bool
144	
145		logLevel string
146	
147		quiet   bool
148		devMode bool // Hidden flag.
149	
150		renderToMemory bool
151	
152		cfgFile string
153		cfgDir  string
154	}
155	
156	// resolveEnvironment sets r.environment if not already set.
157	// server indicates whether the server command is running (defaults to development).
158	func (r *rootCommand) resolveEnvironment(server bool) {
159		if r.environment != "" {
160			return
161		}
162		if env := os.Getenv("HUGO_ENVIRONMENT"); env != "" {
163			r.environment = env
164		} else if env := os.Getenv("HUGO_ENV"); env != "" {
165			r.environment = env
166		} else if server {
167			r.environment = hugo.EnvironmentDevelopment
168		} else {
169			r.environment = hugo.EnvironmentProduction
170		}
171	}
172	
173	func (r *rootCommand) isVerbose() bool {
174		return r.logger.Level() <= logg.LevelInfo
175	}
176	
177	func (r *rootCommand) Close() error {
178		if r.hugoSites != nil {
179			r.hugoSites.DeleteFunc(func(key configKey, value *hugolib.HugoSites) bool {
180				if value != nil {
181					value.Close()
182				}
183				return false
184			})
185		}
186		return nil
187	}
188	
189	func (r *rootCommand) Build(cd *simplecobra.Commandeer, bcfg hugolib.BuildCfg, cfg config.Provider) (*hugolib.HugoSites, error) {
190		h, err := r.Hugo(cfg)
191		if err != nil {
192			return nil, err
193		}
194		if err := h.Build(bcfg); err != nil {
195			return nil, err
196		}
197	
198		return h, nil
199	}
200	
201	func (r *rootCommand) Commands() []simplecobra.Commander {
202		return r.commands
203	}
204	
205	func (r *rootCommand) ConfigFromConfig(key configKey, oldConf *commonConfig) (*commonConfig, error) {
206		cc, _, err := r.commonConfigs.GetOrCreate(key, func(key configKey) (*commonConfig, error) {
207			fs := oldConf.fs
208			configs, err := allconfig.LoadConfig(
209				allconfig.ConfigSourceDescriptor{
210					Flags:                    oldConf.cfg,
211					Fs:                       fs.Source,
212					Filename:                 r.cfgFile,
213					ConfigDir:                r.cfgDir,
214					Logger:                   r.logger,
215					Environment:              r.environment,
216					IgnoreModuleDoesNotExist: key.ignoreModulesDoesNotExists,
217					SkipNpmCheck:             key.skipNpmCheck,
218				},
219			)
220			if err != nil {
221				return nil, err
222			}
223	
224			if !configs.Base.C.Clock.IsZero() {
225				// TODO(bep) find a better place for this.
226				htime.Clock = clocks.Start(configs.Base.C.Clock)
227			}
228	
229			return &commonConfig{
230				mu:      oldConf.mu,
231				configs: configs,
232				cfg:     oldConf.cfg,
233				fs:      fs,
234			}, nil
235		})
236	
237		return cc, err
238	}
239	
240	func (r *rootCommand) ConfigFromProvider(key configKey, cfg config.Provider) (*commonConfig, error) {
241		if cfg == nil {
242			panic("cfg must be set")
243		}
244		r.resolveEnvironment(false)
245		cc, _, err := r.commonConfigs.GetOrCreate(key, func(key configKey) (*commonConfig, error) {
246			var dir string
247			if r.source != "" {
248				dir, _ = filepath.Abs(r.source)
249			} else {
250				dir, _ = os.Getwd()
251			}
252	
253			if cfg == nil {
254				cfg = config.New()
255			}
256	
257			if !cfg.IsSet("workingDir") {
258				cfg.Set("workingDir", dir)
259			} else {
260				if err := os.MkdirAll(cfg.GetString("workingDir"), 0o777); err != nil {
261					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create workingDir: %w", err)
262				}
263			}
264	
265			// Load the config first to allow publishDir to be configured in config file.
266			configs, err := allconfig.LoadConfig(
267				allconfig.ConfigSourceDescriptor{
268					Flags:                    cfg,
269					Fs:                       hugofs.Os,
270					Filename:                 r.cfgFile,
271					ConfigDir:                r.cfgDir,
272					Environment:              r.environment,
273					Logger:                   r.logger,
274					IgnoreModuleDoesNotExist: key.ignoreModulesDoesNotExists,
275					SkipNpmCheck:             key.skipNpmCheck,
276				},
277			)
278			if err != nil {
279				return nil, err
280			}
281	
282			base := configs.Base
283	
284			cfg.Set("publishDir", base.PublishDir)
285			cfg.Set("publishDirStatic", base.PublishDir)
286			cfg.Set("publishDirDynamic", base.PublishDir)
287	
288			renderStaticToDisk := cfg.GetBool("renderStaticToDisk")
289	
290			sourceFs := hugofs.Os
291			var destinationFs afero.Fs
292			if cfg.GetBool("renderToMemory") {
293				destinationFs = afero.NewMemMapFs()
294				if renderStaticToDisk {
295					// Hybrid, render dynamic content to Root.
296					cfg.Set("publishDirDynamic", "/")
297				} else {
298					// Rendering to memoryFS, publish to Root regardless of publishDir.
299					cfg.Set("publishDirDynamic", "/")
300					cfg.Set("publishDirStatic", "/")

61	// HugoSites represents the sites to build. Each site represents a language.
62	type HugoSites struct {
63		// The current sites slice.

96		// that could be overridden in front matter.
97		// Now the different page dimensions (e.g. language) are built-in to the page trees above.
98		// But we sill need to support the overridden translationKey, but that should
99		// be relatively rare and low volume.
100		translationKeyPages *hmaps.SliceCache[page.Page]

111		printSiteBuildDraftsDeprecationInit sync.Once
112		printSiteLanguagesDeprecationInit   sync.Once
113	
114		// File change events with filename stored in this map will be skipped.
115		skipRebuildForFilenamesMu sync.Mutex
116		skipRebuildForFilenames   map[string]bool
117	
118		init *hugoSitesInit

136		return slices.Collect(sp.h.allSitesInterface(nil))

306			fs := hugofs.NewFromSourceAndDestination(sourceFs, destinationFs, cfg)
307	
308			if renderStaticToDisk {
309				dynamicFs := fs.PublishDir
310				publishDirStatic := cfg.GetString("publishDirStatic")
311				workingDir := cfg.GetString("workingDir")
312				absPublishDirStatic := paths.AbsPathify(workingDir, publishDirStatic)
313				staticFs := hugofs.NewBasePathFs(afero.NewOsFs(), absPublishDirStatic)
314	
315				// Serve from both the static and dynamic fs,
316				// the first will take priority.
317				// THis is a read-only filesystem,
318				// we do all the writes to
319				// fs.Destination and fs.DestinationStatic.
320				fs.PublishDirServer = overlayfs.New(
321					overlayfs.Options{
322						Fss: []afero.Fs{
323							dynamicFs,
324							staticFs,
325						},
326					},
327				)
328				fs.PublishDirStatic = staticFs
329	
330			}
331	
332			if !base.C.Clock.IsZero() {
333				// TODO(bep) find a better place for this.
334				htime.Clock = clocks.Start(configs.Base.C.Clock)
335			}
336	
337			if base.PrintPathWarnings {
338				// Note that we only care about the "dynamic creates" here,
339				// so skip the static fs.
340				fs.PublishDir = hugofs.NewCreateCountingFs(fs.PublishDir)
341			}
342	
343			commonConfig := &commonConfig{
344				mu:      &sync.Mutex{},
345				configs: configs,
346				cfg:     cfg,
347				fs:      fs,
348			}
349	
350			return commonConfig, nil
351		})
352	
353		return cc, err
354	}
355	
356	func (r *rootCommand) HugFromConfig(conf *commonConfig) (*hugolib.HugoSites, error) {
357		if conf == nil {
358			return nil, fmt.Errorf("conf must be set")
359		}
360		k := configKey{counter: r.configVersionID.Load()}
361		h, _, err := r.hugoSites.GetOrCreate(k, func(key configKey) (*hugolib.HugoSites, error) {
362			depsCfg := r.newDepsConfig(conf)
363			return hugolib.NewHugoSites(depsCfg)
364		})
365		return h, err
366	}

368	func (r *rootCommand) Hugo(cfg config.Provider) (*hugolib.HugoSites, error) {
369		return r.getOrCreateHugo(cfg, false)
370	}

372	func (r *rootCommand) getOrCreateHugo(cfg config.Provider, ignoreModuleDoesNotExist bool) (*hugolib.HugoSites, error) {
373		k := configKey{counter: r.configVersionID.Load(), ignoreModulesDoesNotExists: ignoreModuleDoesNotExist}
374		h, _, err := r.hugoSites.GetOrCreate(k, func(key configKey) (*hugolib.HugoSites, error) {
375			conf, err := r.ConfigFromProvider(key, cfg)
376			if err != nil {
377				return nil, err
378			}
379			depsCfg := r.newDepsConfig(conf)
380			return hugolib.NewHugoSites(depsCfg)
381		})
382		return h, err
383	}

385	func (r *rootCommand) newDepsConfig(conf *commonConfig) deps.DepsCfg {
386		return deps.DepsCfg{Configs: conf.configs, Fs: conf.fs, StdOut: r.logger.StdOut(), StdErr: r.logger.StdErr(), LogLevel: r.logger.Level(), ChangesFromBuild: r.changesFromBuild}
387	}
388	
389	func (r *rootCommand) Name() string {
390		return "hugo"
391	}
392	
393	func (r *rootCommand) Run(ctx context.Context, cd *simplecobra.Commandeer, args []string) error {
394		b := newHugoBuilder(r, nil)
395	
396		if !r.buildWatch {
397			defer b.postBuild("Total", time.Now())
398		}
399	
400		if err := b.loadConfig(cd, false); err != nil {
401			return err
402		}
403	
404		err := func() error {
405			if r.buildWatch {
406				defer r.timeTrack(time.Now(), "Built")
407			}
408			err := b.build()
409			if err != nil {
410				return err
411			}
412			return nil
413		}()
414		if err != nil {
415			return err
416		}
417	
418		if !r.buildWatch {
419			// Done.
420			return nil
421		}
422	
423		watchDirs, err := b.getDirList()
424		if err != nil {
425			return err
426		}
427	
428		watchGroups := helpers.ExtractAndGroupRootPaths(watchDirs)
429	
430		r.Printf("Watching for changes in %s\n", strings.Join(watchGroups, ", "))
431		watcher, err := b.newWatcher(r.poll, watchDirs...)
432		if err != nil {
433			return err
434		}
435	
436		defer watcher.Close()
437	
438		r.Println("Press Ctrl+C to stop")
439	
440		sigs := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
441		signal.Notify(sigs, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
442	
443		<-sigs
444	
445		return nil
446	}
447	
448	func (r *rootCommand) PreRun(cd, runner *simplecobra.Commandeer) error {
449		r.StdOut = os.Stdout
450		r.StdErr = os.Stderr
451		if r.quiet {
452			r.StdOut = io.Discard
453			r.StdErr = io.Discard
454		}
455		// Used by mkcert (server).
456		log.SetOutput(r.StdOut)
457	
458		r.Printf = func(format string, v ...any) {
459			if !r.quiet {
460				fmt.Fprintf(r.StdOut, format, v...)
461			}
462		}
463		r.Println = func(a ...any) {
464			if !r.quiet {
465				fmt.Fprintln(r.StdOut, a...)
466			}
467		}
468		_, running := runner.Command.(*serverCommand)
469		var err error
470		r.logger, err = r.createLogger(running)
471		if err != nil {
472			return err
473		}
474		// Set up the global logger early to allow info deprecations during config load.
475		loggers.SetGlobalLogger(r.logger)
476	
477		r.changesFromBuild = make(chan []identity.Identity, 10)
478	
479		r.commonConfigs = lazycache.New(lazycache.Options[configKey, *commonConfig]{MaxEntries: 5})
480		// We don't want to keep stale HugoSites in memory longer than needed.
481		r.hugoSites = lazycache.New(lazycache.Options[configKey, *hugolib.HugoSites]{
482			MaxEntries: 1,
483			OnEvict: func(key configKey, value *hugolib.HugoSites) {
484				value.Close()
485				runtime.GC()
486			},
487		})
488	
489		return nil
490	}
491	
492	func (r *rootCommand) createLogger(running bool) (loggers.Logger, error) {
493		level := logg.LevelWarn
494	
495		if r.devMode {
496			level = logg.LevelTrace
497		} else {
498			if r.logLevel != "" {
499				switch strings.ToLower(r.logLevel) {
500				case "debug":
501					level = logg.LevelDebug
502				case "info":
503					level = logg.LevelInfo
504				case "warn", "warning":
505					level = logg.LevelWarn
506				case "error":
507					level = logg.LevelError
508				default:
509					return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid log level: %q, must be one of debug, warn, info or error", r.logLevel)
510				}
511			}
512		}
513	
514		var logHookLast func(e *logg.Entry) error
515		if r.panicOnWarning {
516			logHookLast = loggers.PanicOnWarningHook
517		}
518	
519		optsLogger := loggers.Options{
520			DistinctLevel: logg.LevelWarn,
521			Level:         level,
522			StdOut:        r.StdOut,
523			StdErr:        r.StdErr,
524			StoreErrors:   running,
525			HandlerPost:   logHookLast,
526		}
527	
528		return loggers.New(optsLogger), nil
529	}
530	
531	func (r *rootCommand) resetLogs() {
532		r.logger.Reset()
533		loggers.Log().Reset()
534	}

536	// IsTestRun reports whether the command is running as a test.
537	func (r *rootCommand) IsTestRun() bool {
538		return os.Getenv("HUGO_TESTRUN") != ""
539	}

542		return r.initRootCommand("", cd)

545	func (r *rootCommand) initRootCommand(subCommandName string, cd *simplecobra.Commandeer) error {
546		cmd := cd.CobraCommand
547		commandName := "hugo"
548		if subCommandName != "" {
549			commandName = subCommandName
550		}
551		cmd.Use = fmt.Sprintf("%s [flags]", commandName)
552		cmd.Short = "Build your project"
553		cmd.Long = `COMMAND_NAME is the main command, used to build your Hugo project.
554	
555	Hugo is a Fast and Flexible Static Site Generator
556	built with love by spf13 and friends in Go.
557	
558	Complete documentation is available at https://gohugo.io/.`
559	
560		cmd.Long = strings.ReplaceAll(cmd.Long, "COMMAND_NAME", commandName)
561	
562		// Configure persistent flags
563		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringVarP(&r.source, "source", "s", "", "filesystem path to read files relative from")
564		_ = cmd.MarkFlagDirname("source")
565		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringP("destination", "d", "", "filesystem path to write files to")
566		_ = cmd.MarkFlagDirname("destination")
567	
568		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringVarP(&r.environment, "environment", "e", "", "build environment")
569		_ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc("environment", cobra.NoFileCompletions)
570		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringP("themesDir", "", "", "filesystem path to themes directory")
571		_ = cmd.MarkFlagDirname("themesDir")
572		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringP("ignoreVendorPaths", "", "", "ignores any _vendor for module paths matching the given Glob pattern")
573		cmd.PersistentFlags().BoolP("noBuildLock", "", false, "don't create .hugo_build.lock file")
574		_ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc("ignoreVendorPaths", cobra.NoFileCompletions)
575		cmd.PersistentFlags().String("clock", "", "set the clock used by Hugo, e.g. --clock 2021-11-06T22:30:00.00+09:00")
576		_ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc("clock", cobra.NoFileCompletions)
577	
578		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&r.cfgFile, "config", "", "config file (default is hugo.yaml|json|toml)")
579		_ = cmd.MarkFlagFilename("config", config.ValidConfigFileExtensions...)
580		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&r.cfgDir, "configDir", "config", "config dir")
581		_ = cmd.MarkFlagDirname("configDir")
582		cmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&r.quiet, "quiet", false, "build in quiet mode")
583		cmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVarP(&r.renderToMemory, "renderToMemory", "M", false, "render to memory (mostly useful when running the server)")
584	
585		cmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVarP(&r.devMode, "devMode", "", false, "only used for internal testing, flag hidden.")
586		cmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&r.logLevel, "logLevel", "", "log level (debug|info|warn|error)")
587		_ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc("logLevel", cobra.FixedCompletions([]string{"debug", "info", "warn", "error"}, cobra.ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp))
588		cmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&r.buildWatch, "watch", "w", false, "watch filesystem for changes and recreate as needed")
589	
590		cmd.PersistentFlags().MarkHidden("devMode")
591	
592		// Configure local flags
593		applyLocalFlagsBuild(cmd, r)
594	
595		return nil
596	}

598	// A sub set of the complete build flags. These flags are used by new and mod.
599	func applyLocalFlagsBuildConfig(cmd *cobra.Command, r *rootCommand) {
600		cmd.Flags().StringSliceP("theme", "t", []string{}, "themes to use (located in /themes/THEMENAME/)")
601		_ = cmd.MarkFlagDirname("theme")
602		cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&r.baseURL, "baseURL", "b", "", "hostname (and path) to the root, e.g. https://spf13.com/")

605		cmd.Flags().StringP("contentDir", "c", "", "filesystem path to content directory")
606		cmd.Flags().StringSliceP("renderSegments", "", []string{}, "named segments to render (configured in the segments config)")
607	}

621		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&r.poll, "poll", "", "set this to a poll interval, e.g --poll 700ms, to use a poll based approach to watch for file system changes")
622		_ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc("poll", cobra.NoFileCompletions)
623		cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&r.panicOnWarning, "panicOnWarning", false, "panic on first WARNING log")
624		cmd.Flags().Bool("templateMetrics", false, "display metrics about template executions")
625		cmd.Flags().Bool("templateMetricsHints", false, "calculate some improvement hints when combined with --templateMetrics")
626		cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&r.forceSyncStatic, "forceSyncStatic", false, "copy all files when static is changed.")
627		cmd.Flags().BoolP("noTimes", "", false, "don't sync modification time of files")
628		cmd.Flags().BoolP("noChmod", "", false, "don't sync permission mode of files")
629		cmd.Flags().BoolP("printI18nWarnings", "", false, "print missing translations")
630		cmd.Flags().BoolP("printPathWarnings", "", false, "print warnings on duplicate target paths etc.")
631		cmd.Flags().BoolP("printUnusedTemplates", "", false, "print warnings on unused templates.")
632		cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&r.cpuprofile, "profile-cpu", "", "", "write cpu profile to `file`")
633		cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&r.memprofile, "profile-mem", "", "", "write memory profile to `file`")
634		cmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&r.printm, "printMemoryUsage", "", false, "print memory usage to screen at intervals")
635		cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&r.mutexprofile, "profile-mutex", "", "", "write Mutex profile to `file`")
636		cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&r.traceprofile, "trace", "", "", "write trace to `file` (not useful in general)")
637	
638		// Hide these for now.

643		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("disableKinds", []string{}, "disable different kind of pages (home, RSS etc.)")
644		_ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc("disableKinds", cobra.FixedCompletions(kinds.AllKinds, cobra.ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp))
645		cmd.Flags().Bool("minify", false, "minify any supported output format (HTML, XML etc.)")
646	}

13	
14	// Package minifiers contains minifiers mapped to MIME types. This package is used
15	// in both the resource transformation, i.e. resources.Minify, and in the publishing
16	// chain.
17	package minifiers
18	
19	import (
20		"io"
21		"regexp"
22	
23		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config"
24		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/output"
25		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/transform"
26	
27		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/media"
28		"github.com/tdewolff/minify/v2"
29	)
30	
31	// Client wraps a minifier.
32	type Client struct {
33		// Whether output minification is enabled (HTML in /public)
34		MinifyOutput bool
35	
36		m *minify.M
37	}
38	
39	// Transformer returns a func that can be used in the transformer publishing chain.
40	// TODO(bep) minify config etc
41	func (m Client) Transformer(mediatype media.Type) transform.Transformer {
42		_, params, min := m.m.Match(mediatype.Type)
43		if min == nil {
44			// No minifier for this MIME type
45			return nil
46		}
47	
48		return func(ft transform.FromTo) error {
49			// Note that the source io.Reader will already be buffered, but it implements
50			// the Bytes() method, which is recognized by the Minify library.
51			return min.Minify(m.m, ft.To(), ft.From(), params)
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// Minify tries to minify the src into dst given a MIME type.
56	func (m Client) Minify(mediatype media.Type, dst io.Writer, src io.Reader) error {
57		return m.m.Minify(mediatype.Type, dst, src)
58	}
59	
60	// noopMinifier implements minify.Minifier [1], but doesn't minify content. This means
61	// that we can avoid missing minifiers for any MIME types in our minify.M, which
62	// causes minify to return errors, while still allowing minification to be
63	// disabled for specific types.
64	//
65	// [1]: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/tdewolff/minify#Minifier
66	type noopMinifier struct{}
67	
68	// Minify copies r into w without transformation.
69	func (m noopMinifier) Minify(_ *minify.M, w io.Writer, r io.Reader, _ map[string]string) error {
70		_, err := io.Copy(w, r)
71		return err
72	}
73	
74	// New creates a new Client with the provided MIME types as the mapping foundation.
75	// The HTML minifier is also registered for additional HTML types (AMP etc.) in the
76	// provided list of output formats.
77	func New(mediaTypes media.Types, outputFormats output.Formats, cfg config.AllProvider) (Client, error) {
78		conf := cfg.GetConfigSection("minify").(MinifyConfig)
79		m := minify.New()
80	
81		// We use the Type definition of the media types defined in the site if found.
82		addMinifier(m, mediaTypes, "css", getMinifier(conf, "css"))
83	
84		addMinifier(m, mediaTypes, "js", getMinifier(conf, "js"))
85		m.AddRegexp(regexp.MustCompile("^(application|text)/(x-)?(java|ecma)script$"), getMinifier(conf, "js"))
86	
87		addMinifier(m, mediaTypes, "json", getMinifier(conf, "json"))
88		m.AddRegexp(regexp.MustCompile(`^(application|text)/(x-|(ld|manifest)\+)?json$`), getMinifier(conf, "json"))
89	
90		addMinifier(m, mediaTypes, "svg", getMinifier(conf, "svg"))
91	
92		addMinifier(m, mediaTypes, "xml", getMinifier(conf, "xml"))
93	
94		// HTML
95		addMinifier(m, mediaTypes, "html", getMinifier(conf, "html"))
96		for _, of := range outputFormats {
97			if of.IsHTML {
98				m.Add(of.MediaType.Type, getMinifier(conf, "html"))
99			}
100		}
101	
102		return Client{m: m, MinifyOutput: conf.MinifyOutput}, nil
103	}
104	
105	// getMinifier returns the appropriate minify.MinifierFunc for the MIME
106	// type suffix s, given the config c.
107	func getMinifier(c MinifyConfig, s string) minify.Minifier {
108		switch {
109		case s == "css" && !c.DisableCSS:
110			return &c.Tdewolff.CSS
111		case s == "js" && !c.DisableJS:
112			return &c.Tdewolff.JS
113		case s == "json" && !c.DisableJSON:
114			return &c.Tdewolff.JSON
115		case s == "svg" && !c.DisableSVG:
116			return &c.Tdewolff.SVG
117		case s == "xml" && !c.DisableXML:
118			return &c.Tdewolff.XML
119		case s == "html" && !c.DisableHTML:
120			return &c.Tdewolff.HTML
121		default:
122			return noopMinifier{}
123		}
124	}
125	
126	func addMinifier(m *minify.M, mt media.Types, suffix string, min minify.Minifier) {
127		types := mt.BySuffix(suffix)
128		for _, t := range types {
129			m.Add(t.Type, min)
130		}
131	}

62	// Build builds all sites. If filesystem events are provided,
63	// this is considered to be a potential partial rebuild.
64	func (h *HugoSites) Build(config BuildCfg, events ...fsnotify.Event) error {
65		if h.isRebuild() && !h.Conf.Watching() && !h.Conf.Running() {
66			return errors.New("Build called multiple times when not in watch or server mode (typically with hugolib.Test(t, files).Build(); Build() is already called once by Test)")
67		}
68		if !h.isRebuild() && terminal.PrintANSIColors(os.Stdout) {
69			// Don't show progress for fast builds.
70			d := debounce.New(250 * time.Millisecond)
71			d(func() {
72				h.progressReporter.Start()
73				h.reportProgress(func() (state terminal.ProgressState, progress float64) {
74					// We don't know how many files to process below, so use the intermediate state as the first progress.
75					return terminal.ProgressIntermediate, 1.0
76				})
77			})
78			defer d(func() {})
79		}
80	
81		infol := h.Log.InfoCommand("build")
82		defer loggers.TimeTrackf(infol, time.Now(), nil, "")
83		defer func() {
84			h.reportProgress(func() (state terminal.ProgressState, progress float64) {
85				return terminal.ProgressHidden, 1.0
86			})
87			h.BuildState.BuildCounter.Add(1)
88		}()
89	
90		if h.Deps == nil {
91			panic("must have deps")
92		}
93	
94		if !config.NoBuildLock {
95			unlock, err := h.BaseFs.LockBuild()
96			if err != nil {
97				return fmt.Errorf("failed to acquire a build lock: %w", err)
98			}
99			defer unlock()
100		}

64	// InternalConfig is the internal configuration for Hugo, not read from any user provided config file.
65	type InternalConfig struct {
66		// Server mode?
67		Running bool
68	
69		Quiet          bool
70		Verbose        bool
71		Clock          string
72		Watch          bool
73		FastRenderMode bool
74		LiveReloadPort int
75	}
76	
77	// All non-params config keys for language.
78	var configLanguageKeys map[string]bool
79	
80	func init() {
81		skip := map[string]bool{
82			"internal":   true,
83			"c":          true,
84			"rootconfig": true,
85		}
86		configLanguageKeys = make(map[string]bool)
87		addKeys := func(v reflect.Value) {
88			for i := range v.NumField() {
89				name := strings.ToLower(v.Type().Field(i).Name)
90				if skip[name] {
91					continue
92				}
93				configLanguageKeys[name] = true
94			}
95		}
96		addKeys(reflect.ValueOf(Config{}))
97		addKeys(reflect.ValueOf(RootConfig{}))
98		addKeys(reflect.ValueOf(config.CommonDirs{}))
99		addKeys(reflect.ValueOf(langs.LanguageConfig{}))
100	}
101	
102	type Config struct {
103		// For internal use only.
104		Internal InternalConfig `mapstructure:"-" json:"-"`
105		// For internal use only.
106		C               *ConfigCompiled `mapstructure:"-" json:"-"`
107		isLanguageClone bool
108	
109		RootConfig
110	
111		// Author information.
112		// Deprecated: Use taxonomies instead.
113		Author map[string]any
114	
115		// Social links.
116		// Deprecated: Use .Site.Params instead.
117		Social map[string]string
118	
119		// The build configuration section contains build-related configuration options.
120		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["build"] }</docsmeta>
121		Build config.BuildConfig `mapstructure:"-"`
122	
123		// The caches configuration section contains cache-related configuration options.
124		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["caches"] }</docsmeta>
125		Caches filecache.Configs `mapstructure:"-"`
126	
127		// The httpcache configuration section contains HTTP-cache-related configuration options.
128		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["httpcache"] }</docsmeta>
129		HTTPCache httpcache.Config `mapstructure:"-"`
130	
131		// The markup configuration section contains markup-related configuration options.
132		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["markup"] }</docsmeta>
133		Markup markup_config.Config `mapstructure:"-"`
134	
135		// ContentTypes are the media types that's considered content in Hugo.
136		ContentTypes *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]media.ContentTypeConfig, media.ContentTypes] `mapstructure:"-"`
137	
138		// The mediatypes configuration section maps the MIME type (a string) to a configuration object for that type.
139		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["mediatypes"], "refs": ["types:media:type"] }</docsmeta>
140		MediaTypes *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]media.MediaTypeConfig, media.Types] `mapstructure:"-"`
141	
142		Imaging *config.ConfigNamespace[images.ImagingConfig, images.ImagingConfigInternal] `mapstructure:"-"`
143	
144		// The outputformats configuration sections maps a format name (a string) to a configuration object for that format.
145		OutputFormats *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]output.OutputFormatConfig, output.Formats] `mapstructure:"-"`
146	
147		// The languages configuration sections maps a language code (a string) to a configuration object for that language.
148		Languages *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]langs.LanguageConfig, langs.LanguagesInternal] `mapstructure:"-"`
149	
150		// The versions configuration section contains the top level versions configuration options.
151		Versions *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]versions.VersionConfig, versions.VersionsInternal] `mapstructure:"-"`
152	
153		// The roles configuration section contains the top level roles configuration options.
154		Roles *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]roles.RoleConfig, roles.RolesInternal] `mapstructure:"-"`
155	
156		// The outputs configuration section maps a Page Kind (a string) to a slice of output formats.
157		// This can be overridden in the front matter.
158		Outputs map[string][]string `mapstructure:"-"`
159	
160		// The cascade configuration section contains the top level front matter cascade configuration options,
161		// a slice of page matcher and params to apply to those pages.
162		Cascade *page.PageMatcherParamsConfigs `mapstructure:"-"`
163	
164		// The segments defines segments for the site. Used for partial/segmented builds.
165		Segments *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]segments.SegmentConfig, *segments.Segments] `mapstructure:"-"`
166	
167		// Menu configuration.
168		// <docsmeta>{"refs": ["config:languages:menus"] }</docsmeta>
169		Menus *config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]navigation.MenuConfig, navigation.Menus] `mapstructure:"-"`
170	
171		// The deployment configuration section contains for hugo deployconfig.
172		Deployment deployconfig.DeployConfig `mapstructure:"-"`
173	
174		// Module configuration.
175		Module modules.Config `mapstructure:"-"`
176	
177		// Front matter configuration.
178		Frontmatter pagemeta.FrontmatterConfig `mapstructure:"-"`
179	
180		// Minification configuration.
181		Minify minifiers.MinifyConfig `mapstructure:"-"`
182	
183		// Permalink configuration.
184		Permalinks page.PermalinksConfig `mapstructure:"-"`
185	
186		// Taxonomy configuration.
187		Taxonomies map[string]string `mapstructure:"-"`
188	
189		// Sitemap configuration.
190		Sitemap config.SitemapConfig `mapstructure:"-"`
191	
192		// Related content configuration.
193		Related related.Config `mapstructure:"-"`
194	
195		// Server configuration.
196		Server config.Server `mapstructure:"-"`
197	
198		// Pagination configuration.
199		Pagination config.Pagination `mapstructure:"-"`
200	
201		// Page configuration.
202		Page config.PageConfig `mapstructure:"-"`
203	
204		// Privacy configuration.
205		Privacy privacy.Config `mapstructure:"-"`
206	
207		// Security configuration.
208		Security security.Config `mapstructure:"-"`
209	
210		// Services configuration.
211		Services services.Config `mapstructure:"-"`
212	
213		// User provided parameters.
214		// <docsmeta>{"refs": ["config:languages:params"] }</docsmeta>
215		Params hmaps.Params `mapstructure:"-"`
216	
217		// UglyURLs configuration. Either a boolean or a sections map.
218		UglyURLs any `mapstructure:"-"`
219	}
220	
221	// Early initialization of config.
222	type configCompiler interface {
223		CompileConfig(logger loggers.Logger) error
224	}
225	
226	// Late initialization of config.
227	type configInitializer interface {
228		InitConfig(logger loggers.Logger, defaultSitesMatrix sitesmatrix.VectorStore, configuredDimensions *sitesmatrix.ConfiguredDimensions) error
229	}
230	
231	func (c Config) cloneForLang() *Config {
232		x := c
233		x.isLanguageClone = true
234		x.C = nil
235		copyStringSlice := func(in []string) []string {
236			if in == nil {
237				return nil
238			}
239			out := make([]string, len(in))
240			copy(out, in)
241			return out
242		}
243	
244		// Copy all the slices to avoid sharing.
245		x.DisableKinds = copyStringSlice(x.DisableKinds)
246		x.DisableLanguages = copyStringSlice(x.DisableLanguages)
247		x.MainSections = copyStringSlice(x.MainSections)
248		x.IgnoreLogs = copyStringSlice(x.IgnoreLogs)
249		x.IgnoreFiles = copyStringSlice(x.IgnoreFiles)
250		x.Theme = copyStringSlice(x.Theme)
251	
252		// Collapse all static dirs to one.
253		x.StaticDir = x.staticDirs()
254		// These will go away soon ...
255		x.StaticDir0 = nil
256		x.StaticDir1 = nil
257		x.StaticDir2 = nil
258		x.StaticDir3 = nil
259		x.StaticDir4 = nil
260		x.StaticDir5 = nil
261		x.StaticDir6 = nil
262		x.StaticDir7 = nil
263		x.StaticDir8 = nil
264		x.StaticDir9 = nil
265		x.StaticDir10 = nil
266	
267		return &x
268	}
269	
270	func (c *Config) CompileConfig(logger loggers.Logger) error {
271		var transientErr error
272		s := c.Timeout
273		if _, err := strconv.Atoi(s); err == nil {
274			// A number, assume seconds.
275			s = s + "s"
276		}
277		timeout, err := time.ParseDuration(s)
278		if err != nil {
279			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse timeout: %s", err)
280		}
281		disabledKinds := make(map[string]bool)
282		for _, kind := range c.DisableKinds {
283			kind = strings.ToLower(kind)
284			if newKind := kinds.IsDeprecatedAndReplacedWith(kind); newKind != "" {
285				logger.Deprecatef(false, "Kind %q used in disableKinds is deprecated, use %q instead.", kind, newKind)
286				// Legacy config.
287				kind = newKind
288			}
289			if kinds.GetKindAny(kind) == "" {
290				logger.Warnf("Unknown kind %q in disableKinds configuration.", kind)
291				continue
292			}
293			disabledKinds[kind] = true
294		}
295		kindOutputFormats := make(map[string]output.Formats)
296		isRssDisabled := disabledKinds["rss"]
297		outputFormats := c.OutputFormats.Config
298		for kind, formats := range c.Outputs {
299			if newKind := kinds.IsDeprecatedAndReplacedWith(kind); newKind != "" {
300				logger.Deprecatef(false, "Kind %q used in outputs configuration is deprecated, use %q instead.", kind, newKind)

451			// Sorted is a snapshot of LanguageConfigs taken at decode time; keep it
452			// in sync so Configs.Init, which reads from Sorted, sees the migrated values.
453			for i, s := range c.Languages.Config.Sorted {
454				if s.Name == k {
455					c.Languages.Config.Sorted[i].LanguageConfig = v
456					break
457				}
458			}
459		}
460	
461		// Legacy privacy values.
462		if c.Privacy.Twitter.Disable {
463			hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("project config key privacy.twitter.disable", "Use privacy.x.disable instead.", "v0.141.0", logger.Logger())
464			c.Privacy.X.Disable = c.Privacy.Twitter.Disable
465		}
466		if c.Privacy.Twitter.EnableDNT {
467			hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("project config key privacy.twitter.enableDNT", "Use privacy.x.enableDNT instead.", "v0.141.0", logger.Logger())
468			c.Privacy.X.EnableDNT = c.Privacy.Twitter.EnableDNT
469		}
470		if c.Privacy.Twitter.Simple {
471			hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("project config key privacy.twitter.simple", "Use privacy.x.simple instead.", "v0.141.0", logger.Logger())
472			c.Privacy.X.Simple = c.Privacy.Twitter.Simple
473		}
474	
475		// Legacy services values.
476		if c.Services.Twitter.DisableInlineCSS {
477			hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("project config key services.twitter.disableInlineCSS", "Use services.x.disableInlineCSS instead.", "v0.141.0", logger.Logger())
478			c.Services.X.DisableInlineCSS = c.Services.Twitter.DisableInlineCSS
479		}
480	
481		// Legacy permalink tokens
482		for _, pc := range c.Permalinks {
483			if strings.Contains(pc.Pattern, ":filename") {
484				hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("the \":filename\" permalink token", "Use \":contentbasename\" instead.", "0.144.0", logger.Logger())
485			}
486			if strings.Contains(pc.Pattern, ":slugorfilename") {
487				hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("the \":slugorfilename\" permalink token", "Use \":slugorcontentbasename\" instead.", "0.144.0", logger.Logger())
488			}
489		}
490	
491		// Legacy render hook values.
492		alternativeDetails := fmt.Sprintf(
493			"Set to %q if previous value was false, or set to %q if previous value was true.",
494			gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedNever,
495			gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedFallback,
496		)
497		if c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Image.EnableDefault != nil {
498			alternative := "Use markup.goldmark.renderHooks.image.useEmbedded instead." + " " + alternativeDetails
499			hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("project config key markup.goldmark.renderHooks.image.enableDefault", alternative, "0.148.0", logger.Logger())
500			if *c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Image.EnableDefault {
501				c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Image.UseEmbedded = gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedFallback
502			} else {
503				c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Image.UseEmbedded = gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedNever
504			}
505		}
506		if c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Link.EnableDefault != nil {
507			alternative := "Use markup.goldmark.renderHooks.link.useEmbedded instead." + " " + alternativeDetails
508			hugo.DeprecateWithLogger("project config key markup.goldmark.renderHooks.link.enableDefault", alternative, "0.148.0", logger.Logger())
509			if *c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Link.EnableDefault {
510				c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Link.UseEmbedded = gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedFallback
511			} else {
512				c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Link.UseEmbedded = gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedNever
513			}
514		}
515	
516		// Validate render hook configuration.
517		renderHookUseEmbeddedModes := []string{
518			gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedAlways,
519			gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedAuto,
520			gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedFallback,
521			gc.RenderHookUseEmbeddedNever,
522		}
523		if !slices.Contains(renderHookUseEmbeddedModes, c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Image.UseEmbedded) {
524			return fmt.Errorf("project config markup.goldmark.renderHooks.image.useEmbedded must be one of %s", helpers.StringSliceToList(renderHookUseEmbeddedModes, "or"))
525		}
526		if !slices.Contains(renderHookUseEmbeddedModes, c.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks.Link.UseEmbedded) {
527			return fmt.Errorf("project config markup.goldmark.renderHooks.link.useEmbedded must be one of %s", helpers.StringSliceToList(renderHookUseEmbeddedModes, "or"))
528		}
529	
530		c.C = &ConfigCompiled{
531			Timeout:             timeout,
532			BaseURL:             baseURL,
533			BaseURLLiveReload:   baseURL,
534			DisabledKinds:       disabledKinds,
535			DisabledLanguages:   disabledLangs,
536			IgnoredLogs:         ignoredLogIDs,
537			KindOutputFormats:   kindOutputFormats,
538			DefaultOutputFormat: defaultOutputFormat,
539			CreateTitle:         helpers.GetTitleFunc(c.TitleCaseStyle),
540			IsUglyURLSection:    isUglyURL,
541			IgnoreFile:          ignoreFile,
542			MainSections:        c.MainSections,
543			Clock:               clock,
544			HTTPCache:           httpCache,
545			transientErr:        transientErr,
546		}
547	
548		for _, s := range allDecoderSetups {
549			if getCompiler := s.getCompiler; getCompiler != nil {
550				if err := getCompiler(c).CompileConfig(logger); err != nil {
551					return err
552				}
553			}
554		}
555	
556		return nil
557	}
558	
559	func (c *Config) IsKindEnabled(kind string) bool {
560		return !c.C.DisabledKinds[kind]
561	}
562	
563	func (c *Config) IsLangDisabled(lang string) bool {
564		return c.C.DisabledLanguages[lang]
565	}
566	
567	// ConfigCompiled holds values and functions that are derived from the config.
568	type ConfigCompiled struct {
569		Timeout             time.Duration
570		BaseURL             urls.BaseURL
571		BaseURLLiveReload   urls.BaseURL
572		ServerInterface     string
573		KindOutputFormats   map[string]output.Formats
574		DefaultOutputFormat output.Format
575		DisabledKinds       map[string]bool
576		DisabledLanguages   map[string]bool
577		IgnoredLogs         map[string]bool
578		CreateTitle         func(s string) string
579		IsUglyURLSection    func(section string) bool
580		IgnoreFile          func(filename string) bool
581		MainSections        []string
582		Clock               time.Time
583		HTTPCache           httpcache.ConfigCompiled
584	
585		// This is set to the last transient error found during config compilation.
586		// With themes/modules we compute the configuration in multiple passes, and
587		// errors with missing output format definitions may resolve itself.
588		transientErr error
589	
590		mu sync.Mutex
591	}
592	
593	// This may be set after the config is compiled.
594	func (c *ConfigCompiled) SetMainSections(sections []string) {
595		c.mu.Lock()
596		defer c.mu.Unlock()
597		c.MainSections = sections
598	}
599	
600	// IsMainSectionsSet returns whether the main sections have been set.
601	func (c *ConfigCompiled) IsMainSectionsSet() bool {
602		c.mu.Lock()
603		defer c.mu.Unlock()
604		return c.MainSections != nil
605	}
606	
607	// This is set after the config is compiled by the server command.
608	func (c *ConfigCompiled) SetServerInfo(baseURL, baseURLLiveReload urls.BaseURL, serverInterface string) {
609		c.BaseURL = baseURL
610		c.BaseURLLiveReload = baseURLLiveReload
611		c.ServerInterface = serverInterface
612	}
613	
614	// RootConfig holds all the top-level configuration options in Hugo
615	type RootConfig struct {
616		// The base URL of the site.
617		// Note that the default value is empty, but Hugo requires a valid URL (e.g. "https://example.com/") to work properly.
618		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["URL"] }</docsmeta>
619		BaseURL string
620	
621		// Whether to build content marked as draft.X
622		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["draft"] }</docsmeta>
623		BuildDrafts bool
624	
625		// Whether to build content with expiryDate in the past.
626		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["expiryDate"] }</docsmeta>
627		BuildExpired bool
628	
629		// Whether to build content with publishDate in the future.
630		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["publishDate"] }</docsmeta>
631		BuildFuture bool
632	
633		// Copyright information.
634		Copyright string
635	
636		// The language to apply to content without any language indicator.
637		DefaultContentLanguage string
638	
639		// By default, we put the default content language in the root and the others below their language ID, e.g. /no/.
640		// Set this to true to put all languages below their language ID.
641		DefaultContentLanguageInSubdir bool
642	
643		// The default content role to use for the site.
644		DefaultContentRole string
645	
646		// Set this to true to put the default role in a subdirectory.
647		DefaultContentRoleInSubdir bool
648	
649		// The default content version to use for the site.
650		DefaultContentVersion string
651	
652		// Set to true to render the default version in a subdirectory.
653		DefaultContentVersionInSubdir bool
654	
655		// The default output format to use for the site.
656		// If not set, we will use the first output format.
657		DefaultOutputFormat string
658	
659		// Disable generation of redirect to the default language when DefaultContentLanguageInSubdir is enabled.
660		// Note that this currently is an alias for DisableDefaultSiteRedirect introduced in v0.154.5.
661		// It's not obvious how a more fine grained setup would work.
662		DisableDefaultLanguageRedirect bool
663	
664		// Disable generation of redirects to the default site when DefaultContentRoleInSubdir or DefaultContentVersionInSubdir or DefaultContentLanguageInSubdir is enabled.
665		// The default site is the site is the combination of defaultContentLanguage, defaultContentVersion and defaultContentRole.
666		DisableDefaultSiteRedirect bool
667	
668		// Disable creation of alias redirect pages.
669		DisableAliases bool
670	
671		// Disable lower casing of path segments.
672		DisablePathToLower bool
673	
674		// Disable page kinds from build.
675		DisableKinds []string
676	
677		// A list of languages to disable.
678		DisableLanguages []string
679	
680		// The named segments to render.
681		// This needs to match the name of the segment in the segments configuration.
682		RenderSegments []string
683	
684		// Disable the injection of the Hugo generator tag on the home page.
685		DisableHugoGeneratorInject bool
686	
687		// Disable live reloading in server mode.
688		DisableLiveReload bool
689	
690		// Enable replacement in Pages' Content of Emoji shortcodes with their equivalent Unicode characters.
691		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["Content", "Unicode"] }</docsmeta>
692		EnableEmoji bool
693	
694		// THe main section(s) of the site.
695		// If not set, Hugo will try to guess this from the content.
696		MainSections []string
697	
698		// Enable robots.txt generation.
699		EnableRobotsTXT bool
700	
701		// When enabled, Hugo will apply Git version information to each Page if possible, which
702		// can be used to keep lastUpdated in synch and to print version information.
703		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["Page"] }</docsmeta>
704		EnableGitInfo bool
705	
706		// Enable to track, calculate and print metrics.
707		TemplateMetrics bool
708	
709		// Enable to track, print and calculate metric hints.
710		TemplateMetricsHints bool
711	
712		// Enable to disable the build lock file.
713		NoBuildLock bool
714	
715		// A list of log IDs to ignore.
716		IgnoreLogs []string
717	
718		// A list of regexps that match paths to ignore.
719		// Deprecated: Use the settings on module imports.
720		IgnoreFiles []string
721	
722		// Ignore cache.
723		IgnoreCache bool
724	
725		// Enable to print greppable placeholders (on the form "[i18n] TRANSLATIONID") for missing translation strings.
726		EnableMissingTranslationPlaceholders bool
727	
728		// Enable to panic on warning log entries. This may make it easier to detect the source.
729		PanicOnWarning bool
730	
731		// The configured environment. Default is "development" for server and "production" for build.
732		Environment string
733	
734		// Deprecated: Use Locale instead.
735		LanguageCode string `json:"-"`
736	
737		// The default locale.
738		Locale string
739	
740		// Enable if the site content has CJK language (Chinese, Japanese, or Korean). This affects how Hugo counts words.
741		HasCJKLanguage bool
742	
743		// Whether to pluralize default list titles.
744		// Note that this currently only works for English, but you can provide your own title in the content file's front matter.
745		PluralizeListTitles bool
746	
747		// Whether to capitalize automatic page titles, applicable to section, taxonomy, and term pages.
748		CapitalizeListTitles bool
749	
750		// Make all relative URLs absolute using the baseURL.
751		// <docsmeta>{"identifiers": ["baseURL"] }</docsmeta>
752		CanonifyURLs bool
753	
754		// Enable this to make all relative URLs relative to content root. Note that this does not affect absolute URLs.
755		RelativeURLs bool
756	
757		// Removes non-spacing marks from composite characters in content paths.
758		RemovePathAccents bool
759	
760		// Whether to track and print unused templates during the build.
761		PrintUnusedTemplates bool
762	
763		// Enable to print warnings for missing translation strings.
764		PrintI18nWarnings bool
765	
766		// ENable to print warnings for multiple files published to the same destination.
767		PrintPathWarnings bool
768	
769		// URL to be used as a placeholder when a page reference cannot be found in ref or relref. Is used as-is.
770		RefLinksNotFoundURL string
771	
772		// When using ref or relref to resolve page links and a link cannot be resolved, it will be logged with this log level.
773		// Valid values are ERROR (default) or WARNING. Any ERROR will fail the build (exit -1).
774		RefLinksErrorLevel string
775	
776		// This will create a menu with all the sections as menu items and all the sections’ pages as “shadow-members”.
777		SectionPagesMenu string
778	
779		// The length of text in words to show in a .Summary.
780		SummaryLength int
781	
782		// The site title.
783		Title string
784	
785		// The theme(s) to use.
786		// See Modules for more a more flexible way to load themes.
787		Theme []string
788	
789		// Timeout for generating page contents, specified as a duration or in seconds.
790		Timeout string
791	
792		// The time zone (or location), e.g. Europe/Oslo, used to parse front matter dates without such information and in the time function.
793		TimeZone string
794	
795		// Set titleCaseStyle to specify the title style used by the title template function and the automatic section titles in Hugo.
796		// It defaults to AP Stylebook for title casing, but you can also set it to Chicago or Go (every word starts with a capital letter).
797		TitleCaseStyle string
798	
799		// The editor used for opening up new content.

139	type siteLanguageVersionRole struct {
140		siteVector        sitesmatrix.Vector
141		isDefaultLanguage bool
142	
143		roleInternal roles.RoleInternal
144		role         roles.Role
145	
146		versionInternal versions.VersionInternal
147		version         versions.Version
148	
149		pageMap *pageMap
150	
151		// Page navigation.
152		*pageFinder
153		siteRefLinker siteRefLinker
154	
155		// Shortcut to the home page. Note that this may be nil if
156		// home page, for some odd reason, is disabled.
157		home *pageState
158	
159		// The last modification date of this site.
160		lastmod time.Time
161	
162		// Lazily loaded site dependencies
163		init *siteInit
164	
165		// The output formats that we need to render this site in. This slice
166		// will be fixed once set.
167		// This will be the union of Site.Pages' outputFormats.
168		// This slice will be sorted.
169		renderFormats output.Formats
170	}
171	
172	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) *siteLanguageVersionRole {
173		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Version] = version
174		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Role] = role
175		s.home = nil
176		s.lastmod = time.Time{}
177		s.init = &siteInit{}
178		return &s
179	}
180	
181	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) Role() roles.Role {
182		return s.role
183	}
184	
185	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) Version() versions.Version {
186		return s.version
187	}

22	
23	// ImageResource represents an image resource.
24	type ImageResource interface {
25		resource.Resource
26		ImageResourceOps
27	}
28	
29	type ImageResourceOps interface {
30		// Height returns the height of the Image.
31		Height() int
32	
33		// Width returns the width of the Image.
34		Width() int
35	
36		// Process applies the given image processing options to the image.
37		Process(spec string) (ImageResource, error)
38	
39		// Crop an image to match the given dimensions without resizing.
40		// You must provide both width and height.
41		// Use the anchor option to change the crop box anchor point.
42		//    {{ $image := $image.Crop "600x400" }}
43		Crop(spec string) (ImageResource, error)
44	
45		// Fill scales the image to the smallest possible size that will cover the specified dimensions in spec,
46		// crops the resized image to the specified dimensions using the given anchor point.
47		// The spec is space delimited, e.g. `200x300 TopLeft`.
48		Fill(spec string) (ImageResource, error)
49	
50		// Fit scales down the image using the given spec.
51		Fit(spec string) (ImageResource, error)
52	
53		// Resize resizes the image to the given spec. If one of width or height is 0, the image aspect
54		// ratio is preserved.
55		Resize(spec string) (ImageResource, error)
56	
57		// Filter applies one or more filters to an Image.
58		//    {{ $image := $image.Filter (images.GaussianBlur 6) (images.Pixelate 8) }}
59		Filter(filters ...any) (ImageResource, error)
60	
61		// Exif returns an ExifInfo object containing Image metadata.
62		// Deprecated: Use Meta() instead.
63		Exif() *meta.ExifInfo
64	
65		// Meta returns a MetaInfo object containing Image metadata from all sources (EXIF, IPTC, XMP).
66		Meta() *meta.MetaInfo
67	
68		// Colors returns a slice of the most dominant colors in an image
69		// using a simple histogram method.
70		Colors() ([]Color, error)
71	
72		// For internal use.
73		DecodeImage() (image.Image, error)
74	}

content.content:
340		dimensionLengths := sitesmatrix.Vector{len(confm.Languages), len(versionsSorted), len(rolesSorted)}
341	
342		pageTrees := &pageTrees{
343			treePages: doctree.New(
344				treeConfig,
345			),
346			treeResources: doctree.New(
347				treeConfig,
348			),
349			treeTaxonomyEntries:           doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*weightedContentNode](dimensionLengths),
350			treePagesFromTemplateAdapters: doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*pagesfromdata.PagesFromTemplate](dimensionLengths),
351		}
352	
353		pageTrees.createMutableTrees()
354	
355		for i, confp := range confm.ConfigLangs() {
356			language := confp.Language().(*langs.Language)
357			if language.Disabled {
358				panic("cannot create site for disabled language: " + language.Lang)
359			}
360			k := language.Lang
361			conf := confm.LanguageConfigMap[k]
362			frontmatterHandler, err := pagemeta.NewFrontmatterHandler(firstSiteDeps.Log, conf.Frontmatter)
363			if err != nil {
364				return nil, err
365			}
366	
367			langs.SetParams(language, conf.Params)
368	
369			s := &Site{
370				conf:               conf,
371				language:           language,
372				frontmatterHandler: frontmatterHandler,
373				store:              hstore.NewScratch(),
374				siteLanguageVersionRole: &siteLanguageVersionRole{
375					isDefaultLanguage: language.IsDefault(),
376					siteVector:        sitesmatrix.Vector{i, 0, 0},
377				},
378			}
379	
380			s.siteWrapped = page.WrapSite(s)
381	
382			if i == 0 {
383				firstSiteDeps.Site = s
384				s.Deps = firstSiteDeps
385			} else {
386				d, err := firstSiteDeps.Clone(s, confp)
387				if err != nil {
388					return nil, err
389				}
390				s.Deps = d
391			}
392	
393			// Set up the main publishing chain.
394			pub, err := publisher.NewDestinationPublisher(
395				firstSiteDeps.ResourceSpec,
396				s.conf.OutputFormats.Config,
397				s.conf.MediaTypes.Config,
398			)
399			if err != nil {
400				return nil, err
401			}
402	
403			s.publisher = pub
404			s.relatedDocsHandler = page.NewRelatedDocsHandler(s.conf.Related)
405			// Site deps end.
406	
407			sites = append(sites, s)
408	
409		}

17		"encoding/json"

26	)

28	const (
29		yamlDelimLf = "---\n"
30		tomlDelimLf = "+++\n"
31	)
32	
33	func InterfaceToConfig(in any, format metadecoders.Format, w io.Writer) error {
34		if in == nil {
35			return errors.New("input was nil")
36		}
37	
38		switch format {
39		case metadecoders.YAML:
40			b, err := metadecoders.MarshalYAML(in)
41			if err != nil {
42				return err
43			}
44	
45			_, err = w.Write(b)
46			return err
47	
48		case metadecoders.TOML:
49			enc := toml.NewEncoder(w)
50			enc.SetIndentTables(true)
51			return enc.Encode(in)
52		case metadecoders.JSON:
53			b, err := json.MarshalIndent(in, "", "   ")
54			if err != nil {
55				return err
56			}
57	
58			_, err = w.Write(b)
59			if err != nil {
60				return err
61			}
62	
63			_, err = w.Write([]byte{'\n'})
64			return err
65		case metadecoders.XML:
66			b, err := xml.AnyXmlIndent(in, "", "\t", "root")
67			if err != nil {
68				return err
69			}
70	
71			_, err = w.Write(b)
72			return err
73		default:
74			return errors.New("unsupported Format provided")
75		}
76	}
77	
78	func InterfaceToFrontMatter(in any, format metadecoders.Format, w io.Writer) error {
79		if in == nil {
80			return errors.New("input was nil")
81		}
82	
83		switch format {
84		case metadecoders.YAML:
85			_, err := w.Write([]byte(yamlDelimLf))
86			if err != nil {
87				return err
88			}
89	
90			err = InterfaceToConfig(in, format, w)
91			if err != nil {
92				return err
93			}
94	
95			_, err = w.Write([]byte(yamlDelimLf))
96			return err
97	
98		case metadecoders.TOML:
99			_, err := w.Write([]byte(tomlDelimLf))
100			if err != nil {
101				return err
102			}
103	
104			err = InterfaceToConfig(in, format, w)
105			if err != nil {
106				return err
107			}
108	
109			_, err = w.Write([]byte(tomlDelimLf))
110			return err
111	
112		default:
113			return InterfaceToConfig(in, format, w)
114		}
115	}

14	package converter
15	
16	import (
17		"bytes"
18		"context"

27		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/tableofcontents"
28		"github.com/spf13/afero"
29	)
30	
31	// ProviderConfig configures a new Provider.
32	type ProviderConfig struct {
33		Conf      config.AllProvider // Project config
34		ContentFs afero.Fs
35		Logger    loggers.Logger
36		Exec      *hexec.Exec
37		highlight.Highlighter
38	}
39	
40	func (p ProviderConfig) MarkupConfig() markup_config.Config {
41		return p.Conf.GetConfigSection("markup").(markup_config.Config)
42	}
43	
44	// ProviderProvider creates converter providers.
45	type ProviderProvider interface {
46		New(cfg ProviderConfig) (Provider, error)
47	}
48	
49	// Provider creates converters.
50	type Provider interface {
51		New(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)
52		Name() string
53	}
54	
55	// NewProvider creates a new Provider with the given name.
56	func NewProvider(name string, create func(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)) Provider {
57		return newConverter{
58			name:   name,
59			create: create,
60		}
61	}
62	
63	type newConverter struct {
64		name   string
65		create func(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)
66	}
67	
68	func (n newConverter) New(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error) {
69		return n.create(ctx)
70	}
71	
72	func (n newConverter) Name() string {
73		return n.name
74	}
75	
76	var NopConverter = new(nopConverter)
77	
78	type nopConverter int
79	
80	func (nopConverter) Convert(ctx RenderContext) (ResultRender, error) {
81		return &bytes.Buffer{}, nil
82	}
83	
84	func (nopConverter) Supports(feature identity.Identity) bool {
85		return false
86	}
87	
88	// Converter wraps the Convert method that converts some markup into
89	// another format, e.g. Markdown to HTML.
90	type Converter interface {
91		Convert(ctx RenderContext) (ResultRender, error)
92	}
93	
94	// ParseRenderer is an optional interface.
95	// The Goldmark converter implements this, and this allows us
96	// to extract the ToC without having to render the content.
97	type ParseRenderer interface {
98		Parse(RenderContext) (ResultParse, error)
99		Render(RenderContext, any) (ResultRender, error)
100	}
101	
102	// ResultRender represents the minimum returned from Convert and Render.
103	type ResultRender interface {
104		Bytes() []byte
105	}
106	
107	// ResultParse represents the minimum returned from Parse.
108	type ResultParse interface {
109		Doc() any
110		TableOfContents() *tableofcontents.Fragments
111	}
112	
113	// DocumentInfo holds additional information provided by some converters.
114	type DocumentInfo interface {
115		AnchorSuffix() string
116	}
117	
118	// TableOfContentsProvider provides the content as a ToC structure.
119	type TableOfContentsProvider interface {
120		TableOfContents() *tableofcontents.Fragments
121	}
122	
123	// AnchorNameSanitizer tells how a converter sanitizes anchor names.
124	type AnchorNameSanitizer interface {
125		SanitizeAnchorName(s string) string
126	}
127	
128	// Bytes holds a byte slice and implements the Result interface.
129	type Bytes []byte
130	
131	// Bytes returns itself
132	func (b Bytes) Bytes() []byte {
133		return b
134	}
135	
136	// DocumentContext holds contextual information about the document to convert.
137	type DocumentContext struct {
138		Document       any              // May be nil. Usually a page.Page
139		DocumentLookup func(uint64) any // May be nil.
140		DocumentID     string
141		DocumentName   string
142		Filename       string
143	}
144	
145	// RenderContext holds contextual information about the content to render.
146	type RenderContext struct {
147		// Ctx is the context.Context for the current Page render.
148		Ctx context.Context
149	
150		// Src is the content to render.
151		Src []byte
152	
153		// SourceInfo holds optional information about the source of the content to render.
154		SourceInfo any
155	
156		// Whether to render TableOfContents.
157		RenderTOC bool
158	
159		// GerRenderer provides hook renderers on demand.
160		GetRenderer hooks.GetRendererFunc
161	}

14	package transform
15	
16	import (
17		"bytes"
18		"io"
19		"os"
20	
21		bp "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/bufferpool"
22		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/herrors"
23		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/hugofs"
24	)
25	
26	// Transformer is the func that needs to be implemented by a transformation step.
27	type Transformer func(ft FromTo) error
28	
29	// BytesReader wraps the Bytes method, usually implemented by bytes.Buffer, and an
30	// io.Reader.
31	type BytesReader interface {
32		// The slice given by Bytes is valid for use only until the next buffer modification.
33		// That is, if you want to use this value outside of the current transformer step,
34		// you need to take a copy.
35		Bytes() []byte
36	
37		io.Reader
38	}
39	
40	// FromTo is sent to each transformation step in the chain.
41	type FromTo interface {
42		From() BytesReader
43		To() io.Writer
44	}
45	
46	// Chain is an ordered processing chain. The next transform operation will
47	// receive the output from the previous.
48	type Chain []Transformer
49	
50	// New creates a content transformer chain given the provided transform funcs.
51	func New(trs ...Transformer) Chain {
52		return trs
53	}
54	
55	// NewEmpty creates a new slice of transformers with a capacity of 20.
56	func NewEmpty() Chain {
57		return make(Chain, 0, 20)
58	}
59	
60	// Implements contentTransformer
61	// Content is read from the from-buffer and rewritten to the to-buffer.
62	type fromToBuffer struct {
63		from *bytes.Buffer
64		to   *bytes.Buffer
65	}
66	
67	func (ft fromToBuffer) From() BytesReader {
68		return ft.from
69	}
70	
71	func (ft fromToBuffer) To() io.Writer {
72		return ft.to
73	}
74	
75	// Apply passes the given from io.Reader through the transformation chain.
76	// The result is written to to.
77	func (c *Chain) Apply(to io.Writer, from io.Reader) error {
78		if len(*c) == 0 {
79			_, err := io.Copy(to, from)
80			return err
81		}
82	
83		b1 := bp.GetBuffer()
84		defer bp.PutBuffer(b1)
85	
86		if _, err := b1.ReadFrom(from); err != nil {
87			return err
88		}
89	
90		b2 := bp.GetBuffer()
91		defer bp.PutBuffer(b2)
92	
93		fb := &fromToBuffer{from: b1, to: b2}
94	
95		for i, tr := range *c {
96			if i > 0 {
97				if fb.from == b1 {
98					fb.from = b2
99					fb.to = b1
100					fb.to.Reset()
101				} else {
102					fb.from = b1
103					fb.to = b2
104					fb.to.Reset()
105				}
106			}
107	
108			if err := tr(fb); err != nil {
109				// Write output to a temp file so it can be read by the user for trouble shooting.
110				filename := "output.html"
111				tempfile, ferr := os.CreateTemp("", "hugo-transform-error")
112				if ferr == nil {
113					filename = tempfile.Name()
114					defer tempfile.Close()
115					_, _ = io.Copy(tempfile, fb.from)
116					return herrors.NewFileErrorFromFile(err, filename, hugofs.Os, nil)
117				}
118				return herrors.NewFileErrorFromName(err, filename).UpdateContent(fb.from, nil)
119	
120			}
121		}
122	
123		_, err := fb.to.WriteTo(to)
124		return err
125	}
126

48		_ urls.RefLinker       = (*ShortcodeWithPage)(nil)
49		_ types.Unwrapper      = (*ShortcodeWithPage)(nil)
50		_ text.Positioner      = (*ShortcodeWithPage)(nil)
51		_ hstore.StoreProvider = (*ShortcodeWithPage)(nil)
52	)
53	
54	// ShortcodeWithPage is the "." context in a shortcode template.
55	type ShortcodeWithPage struct {
56		Params        any
57		Inner         template.HTML
58		Page          page.Page
59		Parent        *ShortcodeWithPage
60		Name          string
61		IsNamedParams bool
62	
63		// Zero-based ordinal in relation to its parent. If the parent is the page itself,
64		// this ordinal will represent the position of this shortcode in the page content.
65		Ordinal int
66	
67		// Indentation before the opening shortcode in the source.
68		indentation string
69	
70		innerDeindentInit sync.Once
71		innerDeindent     template.HTML
72	
73		// pos is the position in bytes in the source file. Used for error logging.
74		posInit   sync.Once
75		posOffset int
76		pos       text.Position
77	
78		store *hstore.Scratch
79	}
80	
81	// InnerDeindent returns the (potentially de-indented) inner content of the shortcode.
82	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) InnerDeindent() template.HTML {
83		if scp.indentation == "" {
84			return scp.Inner
85		}
86		scp.innerDeindentInit.Do(func() {
87			b := bp.GetBuffer()
88			text.VisitLinesAfter(string(scp.Inner), func(s string) {
89				if after, ok := strings.CutPrefix(s, scp.indentation); ok {
90					b.WriteString(after)
91				} else {
92					b.WriteString(s)
93				}
94			})
95			scp.innerDeindent = template.HTML(b.String())
96			bp.PutBuffer(b)
97		})
98	
99		return scp.innerDeindent
100	}
101	
102	// Position returns this shortcode's detailed position. Note that this information
103	// may be expensive to calculate, so only use this in error situations.
104	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Position() text.Position {
105		scp.posInit.Do(func() {
106			if p, ok := mustUnwrapPage(scp.Page).(pageContext); ok {
107				scp.pos = p.posOffset(scp.posOffset)
108			}
109		})
110		return scp.pos
111	}
112	
113	// Site returns information about the current site.
114	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Site() page.Site {
115		return scp.Page.Site()
116	}
117	
118	// Ref is a shortcut to the Ref method on Page. It passes itself as a context
119	// to get better error messages.
120	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Ref(args map[string]any) (string, error) {
121		return scp.Page.RefFrom(args, scp)
122	}
123	
124	// RelRef is a shortcut to the RelRef method on Page. It passes itself as a context
125	// to get better error messages.
126	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) RelRef(args map[string]any) (string, error) {
127		return scp.Page.RelRefFrom(args, scp)
128	}
129	
130	// Store returns this shortcode's Store.
131	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Store() *hstore.Scratch {
132		if scp.store == nil {
133			scp.store = hstore.NewScratch()
134		}
135		return scp.store
136	}
137	
138	// Scratch returns a scratch-pad scoped for this shortcode. This can be used
139	// as a temporary storage for variables, counters etc.
140	// Deprecated: Use Store instead. Note that from the templates this should be considered a "soft deprecation".
141	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Scratch() *hstore.Scratch {
142		return scp.Store()
143	}
144	
145	// Get is a convenience method to look up shortcode parameters by its key.
146	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Get(key any) any {
147		if scp.Params == nil {
148			return nil
149		}
150		if reflect.ValueOf(scp.Params).Len() == 0 {
