53	
54	// Deps holds dependencies used by many.
55	// There will be normally only one instance of deps in play
56	// at a given time, i.e. one per Site built.
57	type Deps struct {
58		// The logger to use.
59		Log loggers.Logger `json:"-"`
60	
61		ExecHelper *hexec.Exec
62	
63		// The file systems to use.
64		Fs *hugofs.Fs `json:"-"`
65	
66		// The PathSpec to use
67		*helpers.PathSpec `json:"-"`
68	
69		// The ContentSpec to use
70		*helpers.ContentSpec `json:"-"`
71	
72		// The SourceSpec to use
73		SourceSpec *source.SourceSpec `json:"-"`
74	
75		// The Resource Spec to use
76		ResourceSpec *resources.Spec
77	
78		// The configuration to use
79		Conf config.AllProvider `json:"-"`
80	
81		// The memory cache to use.
82		MemCache *dynacache.Cache
83	
84		// The translation func to use
85		Translate func(ctx context.Context, translationID string, templateData any) string `json:"-"`
86	
87		// The site building.
88		Site page.Site
89	
90		TemplateStore *tplimpl.TemplateStore
91	
92		// Used in tests
93		OverloadedTemplateFuncs map[string]any
94	
95		TranslationProvider ResourceProvider
96	
97		Metrics metrics.Provider
98	
99		// BuildStartListeners will be notified before a build starts.
100		BuildStartListeners *Listeners[any]
101	
102		// BuildEndListeners will be notified after a build finishes.
103		BuildEndListeners *Listeners[any]
104	
105		// OnChangeListeners will be notified when something changes.
106		OnChangeListeners *Listeners[identity.Identity]
107	
108		// Resources that gets closed when the build is done or the server shuts down.
109		BuildClosers *types.Closers
110	
111		// This is common/global for all sites.
112		BuildState *BuildState
113	
114		// Misc counters.
115		Counters *Counters
116	
117		// Holds RPC dispatchers for Katex etc.
118		// TODO(bep) rethink this re. a plugin setup, but this will have to do for now.
119		WasmDispatchers *warpc.Dispatchers
120	
121		// The JS batcher client.
122		JSBatcherClient js.BatcherClient
123	
124		isClosed bool
125	
126		*globalErrHandler
127	}
128	
129	func (d Deps) Clone(s page.Site, conf config.AllProvider) (*Deps, error) {
130		d.Conf = conf
131		d.Site = s
132		d.ExecHelper = nil
133		d.ContentSpec = nil
134	
135		if err := d.Init(); err != nil {
136			return nil, err
137		}
138	
139		return &d, nil
140	}
141	
142	func (d *Deps) GetTemplateStore() *tplimpl.TemplateStore {
143		return d.TemplateStore
144	}
145	
146	func (d *Deps) Init() error {
147		if d.Conf == nil {
148			panic("conf is nil")
149		}
150	
151		if d.Fs == nil {
152			// For tests.
153			d.Fs = hugofs.NewFrom(afero.NewMemMapFs(), d.Conf.BaseConfig())
154		}
155	
156		if d.Log == nil {
157			d.Log = loggers.NewDefault()
158		}
159	
160		if d.globalErrHandler == nil {
161			d.globalErrHandler = &globalErrHandler{
162				logger: d.Log,
163			}
164		}
165		if d.BuildState == nil {
166			d.BuildState = &BuildState{}
167		}
168		if d.Counters == nil {
169			d.Counters = &Counters{}
170		}
171		if d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions == nil {
172			if d.BuildState.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext == nil {
173				d.BuildState.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext = make(map[tpl.RenderingContext]*DeferredExecutions)
174			}
175			d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions = &DeferredExecutions{
176				Executions:              hmaps.NewCache[string, *tpl.DeferredExecution](),
177				FilenamesWithPostPrefix: hmaps.NewCache[string, bool](),
178			}
179		}
180	
181		if d.BuildStartListeners == nil {
182			d.BuildStartListeners = &Listeners[any]{}
183		}
184	
185		if d.BuildEndListeners == nil {
186			d.BuildEndListeners = &Listeners[any]{}
187		}
188	
189		if d.BuildClosers == nil {
190			d.BuildClosers = &types.Closers{}
191		}
192	
193		if d.OnChangeListeners == nil {
194			d.OnChangeListeners = &Listeners[identity.Identity]{}
195		}
196	
197		if d.Metrics == nil && d.Conf.TemplateMetrics() {
198			d.Metrics = metrics.NewProvider(d.Conf.TemplateMetricsHints())
199		}
200	
201		if d.ExecHelper == nil {
202			d.ExecHelper = hexec.New(d.Conf.GetConfigSection("security").(security.Config), d.Conf.WorkingDir(), d.Log)
203		}
204	
205		if d.MemCache == nil {
206			d.MemCache = dynacache.New(dynacache.Options{Watching: d.Conf.Watching(), Log: d.Log})
207		}
208	
209		if d.PathSpec == nil {
210			hashBytesReceiverFunc := func(name string, match []byte) {
211				s := string(match)
212				switch s {
213				case postpub.PostProcessPrefix:
214					d.BuildState.AddFilenameWithPostPrefix(name)
215				case tpl.HugoDeferredTemplatePrefix:
216					d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions.FilenamesWithPostPrefix.Set(name, true)
217				}
218			}
219	
220			// Skip binary files.
221			mediaTypes := d.Conf.GetConfigSection("mediaTypes").(media.Types)
222			hashBytesShouldCheck := func(name string) bool {
223				ext := strings.TrimPrefix(filepath.Ext(name), ".")
224				return mediaTypes.IsTextSuffix(ext)
225			}
226			d.Fs.PublishDir = hugofs.NewHasBytesReceiver(
227				d.Fs.PublishDir,
228				hashBytesShouldCheck,
229				hashBytesReceiverFunc,
230				[]byte(tpl.HugoDeferredTemplatePrefix),
231				[]byte(postpub.PostProcessPrefix))
232	
233			pathSpec, err := helpers.NewPathSpec(d.Fs, d.Conf, d.Log, nil)
234			if err != nil {
235				return err
236			}
237			d.PathSpec = pathSpec
238		} else {
239			var err error
240			d.PathSpec, err = helpers.NewPathSpec(d.Fs, d.Conf, d.Log, d.PathSpec.BaseFs)
241			if err != nil {
242				return err
243			}
244		}
245	
246		d.ExecHelper.SetNodeReadPaths(d.BaseFs.Assets.RealPaths(""))
247	
248		if d.ContentSpec == nil {
249			contentSpec, err := helpers.NewContentSpec(d.Conf, d.Log, d.Content.Fs, d.ExecHelper)
250			if err != nil {
251				return err
252			}
253			d.ContentSpec = contentSpec
254		}
255	
256		if d.SourceSpec == nil {
257			d.SourceSpec = source.NewSourceSpec(d.PathSpec, nil, d.Fs.Source)
258		}
259	
260		var common *resources.SpecCommon
261		if d.ResourceSpec != nil {
262			common = d.ResourceSpec.SpecCommon
263		}
264	
265		d.Cfg.BaseConfig()
266	
267		fileCaches := d.Cfg.FileCaches().(filecache.Caches)
268		fileCaches.SetResourceFs(d.BaseFs.ResourcesCache)
269	
270		resourceSpec, err := resources.NewSpec(d.PathSpec, common, d.WasmDispatchers, fileCaches, d.MemCache, d.BuildState, d.Log, d, d.ExecHelper, d.BuildClosers, d.BuildState)
271		if err != nil {
272			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create resource spec: %w", err)
273		}
274		d.ResourceSpec = resourceSpec
275	
276		return nil
277	}
278	
279	// TODO(bep) rework this to get it in line with how we manage templates.
280	func (d *Deps) Compile(prototype *Deps) error {
281		var err error
282		if prototype == nil {
283	
284			if err = d.TranslationProvider.NewResource(d); err != nil {
285				return err
286			}
287			return nil
288		}
289	
290		if err = d.TranslationProvider.CloneResource(d, prototype); err != nil {
291			return err
292		}
293	
294		return nil
295	}
296	
297	// MkdirTemp returns a temporary directory path that will be cleaned up on exit.
298	func (d Deps) MkdirTemp(pattern string) (string, error) {
299		filename, err := os.MkdirTemp("", pattern)
300		if err != nil {
301			return "", err
302		}
303		d.BuildClosers.Add(
304			types.CloserFunc(
305				func() error {
306					return os.RemoveAll(filename)
307				},
308			),
309		)
310	
311		return filename, nil
312	}
313	
314	type globalErrHandler struct {
315		logger loggers.Logger
316	
317		// Channel for some "hard to get to" build errors
318		buildErrors chan error
319		// Used to signal that the build is done.
320		quit chan struct{}
321	}
322	
323	// SendError sends the error on a channel to be handled later.
324	// This can be used in situations where returning and aborting the current
325	// operation isn't practical.
326	func (e *globalErrHandler) SendError(err error) {
327		if e.buildErrors != nil {
328			select {
329			case <-e.quit:
330			case e.buildErrors <- err:
331			default:
332			}
333			return
334		}
335		e.logger.Errorln(err)
336	}
337	
338	func (e *globalErrHandler) StartErrorCollector() chan error {
339		e.quit = make(chan struct{})
340		e.buildErrors = make(chan error, 10)
341		return e.buildErrors
342	}
343	
344	func (e *globalErrHandler) StopErrorCollector() {
345		if e.buildErrors != nil {
346			close(e.quit)
347			close(e.buildErrors)
348		}
349	}
350	
351	// Listeners represents an event listener.
352	type Listeners[T any] struct {
353		sync.Mutex
354	
355		// A list of funcs to be notified about an event.
356		// If the return value is true, the listener will be removed.
357		listeners []func(...T) bool
358	}
359	
360	// Add adds a function to a Listeners instance.
361	func (b *Listeners[T]) Add(f func(...T) bool) {
362		if b == nil {
363			return
364		}
365		b.Lock()
366		defer b.Unlock()
367		b.listeners = append(b.listeners, f)
368	}
369	
370	// Notify executes all listener functions.
371	func (b *Listeners[T]) Notify(vs ...T) {
372		b.Lock()
373		defer b.Unlock()
374		temp := b.listeners[:0]
375		for _, notify := range b.listeners {
376			if !notify(vs...) {
377				temp = append(temp, notify)
378			}
379		}
380		b.listeners = temp
381	}
382	
383	// ResourceProvider is used to create and refresh, and clone resources needed.
384	type ResourceProvider interface {
385		NewResource(dst *Deps) error
386		CloneResource(dst, src *Deps) error
387	}
388	
389	func (d *Deps) Close() error {
390		if d.isClosed {
391			return nil
392		}
393		d.isClosed = true
394	
395		if d.MemCache != nil {
396			d.MemCache.Stop()
397		}
398		if d.WasmDispatchers != nil {
399			d.WasmDispatchers.Close()
400		}
401		return d.BuildClosers.Close()
402	}
403	
404	// DepsCfg contains configuration options that can be used to configure Hugo
405	// on a global level, i.e. logging etc.
406	// Nil values will be given default values.
407	type DepsCfg struct {
408		// The logger to use. Only set in some tests.
409		// TODO(bep) get rid of this.
410		TestLogger loggers.Logger
411	
412		// The logging level to use.
413		LogLevel logg.Level
414	
415		// Logging output.
416		StdErr io.Writer
417	
418		// The console output.
419		StdOut io.Writer
420	
421		// The file systems to use
422		Fs *hugofs.Fs
423	
424		// The Site in use
425		Site page.Site
426	
427		Configs *allconfig.Configs
428	
429		// Template handling.
430		TemplateProvider ResourceProvider
431	
432		// i18n handling.
433		TranslationProvider ResourceProvider
434	
435		// Build triggered by the IntegrationTest framework.
436		IsIntegrationTest bool
437	
438		// TestCfg holds configuration used only in tests.
439		// It is a programming error to set this when IsIntegrationTest is not set,
440		// and doing so will panic.
441		TestCfg TestConfig
442	
443		// ChangesFromBuild for changes passed back to the server/watch process.
444		ChangesFromBuild chan []identity.Identity
445	}
446	
447	// TestConfig holds configuration used only in tests.
448	// See DepsCfg.TestCfg.
449	type TestConfig struct {
450		// WarpcMemory, if set, overrides the memory limit in MiB for the WASM based
451		// image processors (WebP and AVIF). Used to provoke memory allocation failures.
452		WarpcMemory int
453	}
454	
455	// IsZero reports whether c holds no test configuration.
456	func (c TestConfig) IsZero() bool {
457		return c == TestConfig{}
458	}
459	
460	// BuildState are state used during a build.
461	type BuildState struct {
462		counter uint64
463	
464		// Tracks invocations of the Build method.
465		BuildCounter atomic.Uint64
466	
467		mu sync.Mutex // protects state below.
468	
469		OnSignalRebuild func(ids ...identity.Identity)
470	
471		// A set of filenames in /public that
472		// contains a post-processing prefix.
473		filenamesWithPostPrefix map[string]bool
474	
475		DeferredExecutions *DeferredExecutions
476	
477		// Deferred executions grouped by rendering context.
478		DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext map[tpl.RenderingContext]*DeferredExecutions
479	}
480	
481	// Misc counters.
482	type Counters struct {
483		// Counter for the math.Counter function.
484		MathCounter atomic.Uint64
485	}
486	
487	type DeferredExecutions struct {
488		// A set of filenames in /public that
489		// contains a post-processing prefix.
490		FilenamesWithPostPrefix *hmaps.Cache[string, bool]
491	
492		// Maps a placeholder to a deferred execution.
493		Executions *hmaps.Cache[string, *tpl.DeferredExecution]
494	}
495	
496	var _ identity.SignalRebuilder = (*BuildState)(nil)
497	
498	// IsRebuild reports whether this is a rebuild.
499	func (b *BuildState) IsRebuild() bool {
500		return b.BuildCounter.Load() > 0
501	}
502	
503	// StartStageRender will be called before a stage is rendered.
504	func (b *BuildState) StartStageRender(stage tpl.RenderingContext) {
505	}
506	
507	// StopStageRender will be called after a stage is rendered.
508	func (b *BuildState) StopStageRender(stage tpl.RenderingContext) {
509		b.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext[stage] = b.DeferredExecutions
510		b.DeferredExecutions = &DeferredExecutions{
511			Executions:              hmaps.NewCache[string, *tpl.DeferredExecution](),
512			FilenamesWithPostPrefix: hmaps.NewCache[string, bool](),
513		}
514	}
515	
516	func (b *BuildState) SignalRebuild(ids ...identity.Identity) {
517		b.OnSignalRebuild(ids...)
518	}
519	
520	func (b *BuildState) AddFilenameWithPostPrefix(filename string) {
521		b.mu.Lock()
522		defer b.mu.Unlock()
523		if b.filenamesWithPostPrefix == nil {
524			b.filenamesWithPostPrefix = make(map[string]bool)
525		}
526		b.filenamesWithPostPrefix[filename] = true
527	}
528	
529	func (b *BuildState) GetFilenamesWithPostPrefix() []string {
530		b.mu.Lock()
531		defer b.mu.Unlock()
532		var filenames []string
533		for filename := range b.filenamesWithPostPrefix {
534			filenames = append(filenames, filename)
535		}
536		sort.Strings(filenames)
537		return filenames
538	}
539	
540	func (b *BuildState) Incr() int {
541		return int(atomic.AddUint64(&b.counter, uint64(1)))
542	}

14	package helpers

17		"strings"

26	// PathSpec holds methods that decides how paths in URLs and files in Hugo should look like.
27	type PathSpec struct {
28		*paths.Paths
29		*filesystems.BaseFs
30	
31		ProcessingStats *ProcessingStats
32	
33		// The file systems to use
34		Fs *hugofs.Fs
35	}
36	
37	// NewPathSpec creates a new PathSpec from the given filesystems and language.
38	// If an existing BaseFs is provided, parts of that is reused.
39	func NewPathSpec(fs *hugofs.Fs, cfg config.AllProvider, logger loggers.Logger, baseBaseFs *filesystems.BaseFs) (*PathSpec, error) {
40		p, err := paths.New(fs, cfg)
41		if err != nil {
42			return nil, err
43		}
44	
45		var options []func(*filesystems.BaseFs) error
46		if baseBaseFs != nil {
47			options = []func(*filesystems.BaseFs) error{
48				filesystems.WithBaseFs(baseBaseFs),
49			}
50		}
51		bfs, err := filesystems.NewBase(p, logger, options...)
52		if err != nil {
53			return nil, err
54		}
55	
56		ps := &PathSpec{
57			Paths:           p,
58			BaseFs:          bfs,
59			Fs:              fs,
60			ProcessingStats: NewProcessingStats(p.Lang()),
61		}
62	
63		return ps, nil
64	}
65	
66	// PermalinkForBaseURL creates a permalink from the given link and baseURL.
67	func (p *PathSpec) PermalinkForBaseURL(link, baseURL string) string {
68		return baseURL + strings.TrimPrefix(link, "/")
69	}

14	// Package filesystems provides the fine grained file systems used by Hugo. These
15	// are typically virtual filesystems that are composites of project and theme content.
16	package filesystems
17	
18	import (
19		"context"
20		"fmt"
21		"io"
22		"os"
23		"path/filepath"
24		"strings"
25		"sync"
26	
27		"github.com/bep/overlayfs"
28		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config"
29		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/htesting"
30		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/langs"
31	
32		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/herrors"
33		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hugo"
34		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/loggers"
35	
36		"github.com/rogpeppe/go-internal/lockedfile"
37	
38		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/hugofs/files"
39		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/hugofs/hglob"
40	
41		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/modules"
42	
43		hpaths "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/paths"
44		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/hugofs"
45		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/hugolib/paths"
46		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/hugolib/sitesmatrix"
47		"github.com/spf13/afero"
48	)
49	
50	const (
51		// Used to control concurrency between multiple Hugo instances, e.g.
52		// a running server and building new content with 'hugo new'.
53		// It's placed in the project root.
54		lockFileBuild = ".hugo_build.lock"
55	)
56	
57	var filePathSeparator = string(filepath.Separator)
58	
59	// BaseFs contains the core base filesystems used by Hugo. The name "base" is used
60	// to underline that even if they can be composites, they all have a base path set to a specific
61	// resource folder, e.g "/my-project/content". So, no absolute filenames needed.
62	type BaseFs struct {
63		// SourceFilesystems contains the different source file systems.
64		*SourceFilesystems
65	
66		// The source filesystem (needs absolute filenames).
67		SourceFs afero.Fs
68	
69		// The project source.
70		ProjectSourceFs afero.Fs
71	
72		// The filesystem used to publish the rendered site.
73		// This usually maps to /my-project/public.
74		PublishFs afero.Fs
75	
76		// The filesystem used for static files.
77		PublishFsStatic afero.Fs
78	
79		// A read-only filesystem starting from the project workDir.
80		WorkDir afero.Fs
81	
82		theBigFs *filesystemsCollector
83	
84		workingDir string
85	
86		// Locks.
87		buildMu Lockable // <project>/.hugo_build.lock
88	}
89	
90	type Lockable interface {
91		Lock() (unlock func(), err error)
92	}
93	
94	type fakeLockfileMutex struct {
95		mu sync.Mutex
96	}
97	
98	func (f *fakeLockfileMutex) Lock() (func(), error) {
99		f.mu.Lock()
100		return func() { f.mu.Unlock() }, nil
101	}
102	
103	// Tries to acquire a build lock.
104	func (b *BaseFs) LockBuild() (unlock func(), err error) {
105		return b.buildMu.Lock()
106	}
107	
108	func (b *BaseFs) WatchFilenames() []string {
109		var filenames []string
110		sourceFs := b.SourceFs
111	
112		for _, rfs := range b.RootFss {
113			for _, component := range files.ComponentFolders {
114				fis, err := rfs.Mounts(component)
115				if err != nil {
116					continue
117				}
118	
119				for _, fim := range fis {
120					meta := fim.Meta()
121					if !meta.Watch {
122						continue
123					}
124	
125					if !fim.IsDir() {
126						filenames = append(filenames, meta.Filename)
127						continue
128					}
129	
130					w := hugofs.NewWalkway(hugofs.WalkwayConfig{
131						Fs:   sourceFs,
132						Root: meta.Filename,
133						WalkFn: func(ctx context.Context, path string, fi hugofs.FileMetaInfo) error {
134							if !fi.IsDir() {
135								return nil
136							}
137							if fi.Name() == ".git" ||
138								fi.Name() == "node_modules" || fi.Name() == "bower_components" {
139								return filepath.SkipDir
140							}
141							filenames = append(filenames, fi.Meta().Filename)
142							return nil
143						},
144					})
145	
146					w.Walk()
147				}
148	
149			}
150		}
151	
152		return filenames
153	}
154	
155	func (b *BaseFs) mountsForComponent(component string) []hugofs.FileMetaInfo {
156		var result []hugofs.FileMetaInfo
157		for _, rfs := range b.RootFss {
158			dirs, err := rfs.Mounts(component)
159			if err == nil {
160				result = append(result, dirs...)
161			}
162		}
163		return result
164	}
165	
166	// AbsProjectContentDir tries to construct a filename below the most
167	// relevant content directory.
168	func (b *BaseFs) AbsProjectContentDir(filename string) (string, string, error) {
169		isAbs := filepath.IsAbs(filename)

171			if !fi.IsDir() {
172				continue
173			}
174			meta := fi.Meta()
175			if !meta.IsProject {
176				continue
177			}
178	
179			if isAbs {
180				if strings.HasPrefix(filename, meta.Filename) {
181					return strings.TrimPrefix(filename, meta.Filename+filePathSeparator), filename, nil
182				}
183			} else {
184				contentDir := strings.TrimPrefix(strings.TrimPrefix(meta.Filename, meta.BaseDir), filePathSeparator) + filePathSeparator
185	
186				if after, ok := strings.CutPrefix(filename, contentDir); ok {
187					relFilename := after
188					absFilename := filepath.Join(meta.Filename, relFilename)
189					return relFilename, absFilename, nil
190				}
191			}
192	
193		}

198			// Pick the first project dir (which is probably the most important one).

29		// Attributes passed in from Markdown (e.g. { attrName1=attrValue1 attrName2="attr Value 2" }).
30		Attributes() map[string]any
31	}
32	
33	// LinkContext is the context passed to a link render hook.
34	type LinkContext interface {
35		PageProvider
36	
37		// The link URL.
38		Destination() string
39	
40		// The link title attribute.
41		Title() string
42	
43		// The rendered (HTML) text.
44		Text() hstring.HTML
45	
46		// The plain variant of Text.
47		PlainText() string
48	}
49	
50	// ImageLinkContext is the context passed to a image link render hook.
51	type ImageLinkContext interface {
52		LinkContext
53	
54		// Returns true if this is a standalone image and the config option
55		// markup.goldmark.parser.wrapStandAloneImageWithinParagraph is disabled.
56		IsBlock() bool
57	
58		// Zero-based ordinal for all the images in the current document.
59		Ordinal() int
60	}
61	
62	// CodeblockContext is the context passed to a code block render hook.
63	type CodeblockContext interface {
64		BaseContext
65		AttributesProvider
66	
67		// Chroma highlighting processing options. This will only be filled if Type is a known Chroma Lexer.
68		Options() map[string]any
69	
70		// The type of code block. This will be the programming language, e.g. bash, when doing code highlighting.
71		Type() string
72	
73		// The text between the code fences.
74		Inner() string
75	}
76	
77	// TableContext is the context passed to a table render hook.
78	type TableContext interface {
79		BaseContext
80		AttributesProvider
81	
82		THead() []TableRow
83		TBody() []TableRow
84	}
85

87	type BaseContext interface {
88		text.Positioner
89		PageProvider
90	
91		// Zero-based ordinal for all elements of this kind in the current document.
92		Ordinal() int
93	}
94	
95	// BlockquoteContext is the context passed to a blockquote render hook.
96	type BlockquoteContext interface {
97		BaseContext
98	
99		AttributesProvider
100	
101		// The blockquote text.
102		// If type is "alert", this will be the alert text.
103		Text() hstring.HTML
104	
105		/// Returns the blockquote type, one of "regular" and "alert".
106		// Type "alert" indicates that this is a GitHub type alert.
107		Type() string
108	
109		// The GitHub alert type converted to lowercase, e.g. "note".
110		// Only set if Type is "alert".
111		AlertType() string
112	
113		// The alert title.
114		// Currently only relevant for Obsidian alerts.
115		// GitHub does not suport alert titles and will not render alerts with titles.
116		AlertTitle() hstring.HTML
117	
118		// The alert sign,  "+" or "-" or "" used to indicate folding.
119		// Currently only relevant for Obsidian alerts.
120		// GitHub does not suport alert signs and will not render alerts with signs.
121		AlertSign() string
122	}
123	
124	// PassThroughContext is the context passed to a passthrough render hook.
125	type PassthroughContext interface {
126		BaseContext
127		AttributesProvider
128	
129		// Currently one of "inline" or "block".
130		Type() string
131	
132		// The inner content of the passthrough element, excluding the delimiters.
133		Inner() string
134	}
135	
136	type AttributesOptionsSliceProvider interface {
137		AttributesSlice() []attributes.Attribute
138		OptionsSlice() []attributes.Attribute
139	}

143	}

147	}

28		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config"
29	
30		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/kinds"
31		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/page"
32	)
33	
34	type siteRenderContext struct {
35		cfg *BuildCfg
36	
37		infol logg.LevelLogger
38	
39		// languageIdx is the zero based index of the site.
40		languageIdx int
41	
42		// Zero based index for all output formats combined.
43		sitesOutIdx int
44	
45		// Zero based index of the output formats configured within a Site.
46		// Note that these outputs are sorted.
47		outIdx int
48	
49		multihost bool
50	}
51	
52	// Whether to render 404.html, robotsTXT.txt and similar.
53	// These are usually rendered once in the root of public.
54	func (s siteRenderContext) shouldRenderStandalonePage(kind string) bool {
55		if s.multihost || kind == kinds.KindSitemap {
56			// 1 per site
57			return s.outIdx == 0
58		}
59	
60		if kind == kinds.KindTemporary || kind == kinds.KindStatus404 {
61			// 1 for all output formats
62			return s.outIdx == 0
63		}
64	
65		// 1 for all sites and output formats.
66		return s.languageIdx == 0 && s.outIdx == 0
67	}
68	
69	// renderPages renders pages concurrently.
70	func (s *Site) renderPages(ctx *siteRenderContext) error {
71		numWorkers := config.GetNumWorkerMultiplier()
72	
73		results := make(chan error)
74		pages := make(chan *pageState, numWorkers) // buffered for performance
75		errs := make(chan error)
76	
77		go s.errorCollator(results, errs)
78	
79		wg := &sync.WaitGroup{}
80	
81		for range numWorkers {
82			wg.Add(1)
83			go pageRenderer(ctx, s, pages, results, wg)
84		}
85	
86		cfg := ctx.cfg
87	
88		w := &doctree.NodeShiftTreeWalker[contentNode]{
89			Tree: s.pageMap.treePages,
90			Handle: func(key string, n contentNode) (radix.WalkFlag, error) {
91				if p, ok := n.(*pageState); ok {
92					if cfg.shouldRender(ctx.infol, p) {
93						select {
94						case <-s.h.Done():
95							return radix.WalkStop, nil
96						default:
97							pages <- p
98						}
99					}

### 1. **RESOURCE PIPELINE - Core Resource Types**

**File:** `/home/bench/repos/hugo/resources/postpub/postpub.go`

  - Field: `prefix` and `delegate` (Line 60-61)

14	package deps
15	
16	import (
17		"context"
18		"fmt"
19		"io"
20		"os"
21		"path/filepath"
22		"sort"

30		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hexec"
31		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hmaps"
32		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/loggers"
33		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/types"
34		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config"
35		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config/allconfig"
36		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config/security"
37		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/helpers"
38		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/hugofs"
39		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/identity"
40		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/internal/js"
41		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/internal/warpc"
42		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/media"
43		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/page"
44		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/postpub"
45		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl/tplimpl"
46	
47		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/metrics"
48		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources"
49		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/source"
50		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl"
51		"github.com/spf13/afero"
52	)
53	
54	// Deps holds dependencies used by many.
55	// There will be normally only one instance of deps in play
56	// at a given time, i.e. one per Site built.
57	type Deps struct {
58		// The logger to use.
59		Log loggers.Logger `json:"-"`
60	
61		ExecHelper *hexec.Exec
62	
63		// The file systems to use.
64		Fs *hugofs.Fs `json:"-"`
65	
66		// The PathSpec to use
67		*helpers.PathSpec `json:"-"`
68	
69		// The ContentSpec to use
70		*helpers.ContentSpec `json:"-"`
71	
72		// The SourceSpec to use
73		SourceSpec *source.SourceSpec `json:"-"`
74	
75		// The Resource Spec to use
76		ResourceSpec *resources.Spec
77	
78		// The configuration to use
79		Conf config.AllProvider `json:"-"`
80	
81		// The memory cache to use.
82		MemCache *dynacache.Cache
83	
84		// The translation func to use
85		Translate func(ctx context.Context, translationID string, templateData any) string `json:"-"`
86	
87		// The site building.
88		Site page.Site
89	
90		TemplateStore *tplimpl.TemplateStore
91	
92		// Used in tests
93		OverloadedTemplateFuncs map[string]any
94	
95		TranslationProvider ResourceProvider
96	
97		Metrics metrics.Provider
98	
99		// BuildStartListeners will be notified before a build starts.
100		BuildStartListeners *Listeners[any]
101	
102		// BuildEndListeners will be notified after a build finishes.
103		BuildEndListeners *Listeners[any]
104	
105		// OnChangeListeners will be notified when something changes.
106		OnChangeListeners *Listeners[identity.Identity]
107	
108		// Resources that gets closed when the build is done or the server shuts down.
109		BuildClosers *types.Closers
110	
111		// This is common/global for all sites.
112		BuildState *BuildState
113	
114		// Misc counters.
115		Counters *Counters
116	
117		// Holds RPC dispatchers for Katex etc.
118		// TODO(bep) rethink this re. a plugin setup, but this will have to do for now.
119		WasmDispatchers *warpc.Dispatchers
120	
121		// The JS batcher client.
122		JSBatcherClient js.BatcherClient
123	
124		isClosed bool
125	
126		*globalErrHandler
127	}
128	
129	func (d Deps) Clone(s page.Site, conf config.AllProvider) (*Deps, error) {
130		d.Conf = conf
131		d.Site = s
132		d.ExecHelper = nil
133		d.ContentSpec = nil
134	
135		if err := d.Init(); err != nil {
136			return nil, err
137		}
138	
139		return &d, nil
140	}
141	
142	func (d *Deps) GetTemplateStore() *tplimpl.TemplateStore {
143		return d.TemplateStore
144	}
145	
146	func (d *Deps) Init() error {
147		if d.Conf == nil {
148			panic("conf is nil")
149		}
150	
151		if d.Fs == nil {
152			// For tests.
153			d.Fs = hugofs.NewFrom(afero.NewMemMapFs(), d.Conf.BaseConfig())
154		}
155	
156		if d.Log == nil {
157			d.Log = loggers.NewDefault()
158		}
159	
160		if d.globalErrHandler == nil {
161			d.globalErrHandler = &globalErrHandler{
162				logger: d.Log,
163			}
164		}
165		if d.BuildState == nil {
166			d.BuildState = &BuildState{}
167		}
168		if d.Counters == nil {
169			d.Counters = &Counters{}
170		}
171		if d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions == nil {
172			if d.BuildState.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext == nil {
173				d.BuildState.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext = make(map[tpl.RenderingContext]*DeferredExecutions)
174			}
175			d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions = &DeferredExecutions{
176				Executions:              hmaps.NewCache[string, *tpl.DeferredExecution](),
177				FilenamesWithPostPrefix: hmaps.NewCache[string, bool](),
178			}
179		}
180	
181		if d.BuildStartListeners == nil {
182			d.BuildStartListeners = &Listeners[any]{}
183		}
184	
185		if d.BuildEndListeners == nil {
186			d.BuildEndListeners = &Listeners[any]{}
187		}
188	
189		if d.BuildClosers == nil {
190			d.BuildClosers = &types.Closers{}
191		}
192	
193		if d.OnChangeListeners == nil {
194			d.OnChangeListeners = &Listeners[identity.Identity]{}
195		}
196	
197		if d.Metrics == nil && d.Conf.TemplateMetrics() {
198			d.Metrics = metrics.NewProvider(d.Conf.TemplateMetricsHints())
199		}
200

content.text:
Based on my thorough exploration, I have gathered comprehensive information about Hugo's core build engine. Let me now compile my final report:

## Core Build Engine Trace - Hugo Codebase

Based on my thorough analysis of /home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/, here is the complete trace of Hugo's core build engine:

### 1. **HugoSites Type**

**File:** `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites.go` (lines 62-127)

**Type Definition:**
```go
type HugoSites struct {
    // The current sites slice.
    // When rendering, this slice will be shifted out.
    Sites []*Site
    
    // All sites for all versions and roles.
    sitesVersionsRoles    [][][]*Site
    sitesVersionsRolesMap map[sitesmatrix.Vector]*Site
    sitesLanguages        []*Site // sample set with all languages.
    
    Configs *allconfig.Configs
    
    hugoInfo page.HugoInfo
    
    // Render output formats for all sites.
    renderFormats output.Formats
    
    // The currently rendered Site.
    currentSite *Site
    
    *deps.Deps
    
    gitInfo       *gitInfo
    codeownerInfo *codeownerInfo
    
    // As loaded from the /data dirs
    data map[string]any
    
    // Cache for page listings.
    cachePages *dynacache.Partition[string, page.Pages]
    // Cache for content sources.
    cacheContentSource *dynacache.Partition[uint64, *resources.StaleValue[[]byte]]
    
    translationKeyPages *hmaps.SliceCache[page.Page]
    
    pageTrees                    *pageTrees
    previousPageTreesWalkContext *doctree.WalkContext[contentNode]
    previousSeenTerms            *maphelpers.ConcurrentMap[term, sitesmatrix.Vectors]
    
    // Various init/state fields...
    init *hugoSitesInit
    
    workersSite     *para.Workers
    numWorkersSites int
    numWorkers      int
    
    *progressReporter
    *fatalErrorHandler
    *buildCounters
}
```

**Key Fields:**
- `Sites`: Slice of current sites (for rendering, may be shifted)
- `sitesVersionsRoles`: 3D matrix of all sites for all language/version/role combinations
- `sitesVersionsRolesMap`: Map to look up sites by their vector (language, version, role)
- `pageTrees`: The content tree structure containing pages, resources, and taxonomy entries
- `Configs`: All configuration for all languages
- `Deps`: Dependencies container (deps.Deps)
- `data`: Map loaded from /data directories
- `cachePages`: Cache for page listings across builds

---

### 2. **Top-Level Build Function**

**File:** `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go` (lines 64-251)

**Function Signature:**
```go
func (h *HugoSites) Build(config BuildCfg, events ...fsnotify.Event) error
```

**Key Parameters:**
- `config BuildCfg`: Build configuration with options like SkipRender, WhatChanged, PartialReRender
- `events`: Optional file system events for partial rebuilds

**Location:** `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:64-251`

---

### 3. **Site Type**

**File:** `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site.go` (lines 103-120)

**Type Definition:**
```go
type Site struct {
    state       siteState
    conf        *allconfig.Config
    language    *langs.Language
    store       *hstore.Scratch
    siteWrapped page.Site
    
    // The owning container.
    h *HugoSites
    
    *deps.Deps
    *siteLanguageVersionRole
    
    relatedDocsHandler *page.RelatedDocsHandler
    
    publisher          publisher.Publisher
    frontmatterHandler pagemeta.FrontMatterHandler
}
```

**Key Fields:**
- `conf`: Configuration for this specific site/language
- `language`: Language details for this site
- `h`: Reference to parent HugoSites container
- `Deps`: Site-specific dependencies
- `siteLanguageVersionRole`: Embedded struct containing pageMap, pageFinder, and version/role info
- `publisher`: Output publisher for writing rendered content
- `frontmatterHandler`: Parses front matter from content files

**siteLanguageVersionRole (embedded) contains:**
- `siteVector`: Position in the language/version/role matrix
- `pageMap *pageMap`: The content tree for this site's pages and resources
- `pageFinder *pageFinder`: Helper for finding pages
- `home *pageState`: Shortcut to home page
- `renderFormats output.Formats`: Output formats for this site

---

### 4. **Constructor for HugoSites**

**File:** `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site.go` (lines 199-460)

**Function Signature:**
```go
func NewHugoSites(cfg deps.DepsCfg) (*HugoSites, error)
```

**Location:** `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site.go:199-460`

**Key Steps:**
1. Creates logger and memory cache
2. Initializes first site dependencies
3. Creates config validators
4. Builds doctree structures for pages, resources, and taxonomies
5. Creates a Site for each language
6. Clones dependencies for multi-language sites
7. Calls `newHugoSites()` helper to finalize HugoSites initialization

**Key Helper:**
```go
func newHugoSites(cfg deps.DepsCfg, d *deps.Deps, pageTrees *pageTrees, sitesVersionsRoles [][][]*Site) (*HugoSites, error)
```
Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site.go:462-621`

---

### 5. **Build Pipeline Phases**

The `HugoSites.Build()` method calls these major phases in sequence (from `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go`):

#### Phase 1: **Initialization**
- Method: `initSites()` or `initRebuild()` 
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:256-279`
- Purpose: Reset build state, setup error handlers

#### Phase 2: **Process (Read & Parse Content)**
- Method: `process()`
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:283-298`
- Calls:
  - `processPartialFileEvents()` - for file change events
  - `processPartialRebuildChanges()` - for remote rebuild signals
  - `processFull()` - for full builds

**processFull Implementation:**
```go
func (h *HugoSites) processFull(ctx context.Context, l logg.LevelLogger, config *BuildCfg) error {
    return h.processFiles(ctx, l, config)
}
```
Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:1274-1280`

**processFiles Implementation:**
- Creates `sourceSpec` for file metadata
- Creates `pagesCollector` via `newPagesCollector()`
- Calls `c.Collect()` to read and parse all content files
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:1346-1363`

#### Phase 3: **Assemble (Create Missing Sections, Apply Aggregates)**
- Method: `assemble()`
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:302-396`
- Calls:
  - `createAllPages()` - Creates page tree structure
  - `assemblePagesStep1()` - First assembly phase
  - `assemblePagesStep2()` - Second assembly phase (handles cascade)
  - `initRenderFormats()` - Determines output formats
  - `assemblePagesStepFinal()` - Final assembly phase

#### Phase 4: **Render (Generate Output)**
- Method: `render()`
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:399-486`
- For each site and output format:
  - Calls `s.preparePagesForRender()` to shift pages to correct output format
  - Calls `s.render()` or `s.renderPages()` to generate HTML/other output
  - Uses `siteRenderContext` to track render state

#### Phase 5: **Render Deferred (Handle Async Templates)**
- Method: `renderDeferred()`
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:488-517`
- Executes templates deferred during rendering

#### Phase 6: **Post-Process (CSS, JS, Stats)**
- Method: `postProcess()`
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go:650-766`
- Runs post-processors for resources like JS/CSS transforms
- Also: `writeBuildStats()` writes hugo_stats.json

---

### 6. **Content Tree Structure (pageTrees & pageMap)**

**pageTrees Type (defined in site.go:342-351):**
```go
type pageTrees struct {
    treePages                      doctree.Tree[contentNode]  // Pages indexed by path
    treeResources                  doctree.Tree[contentNode]  // Resources indexed by path
    treeTaxonomyEntries            doctree.TreeShiftTree[*weightedContentNode]  // Taxonomy terms
    treePagesFromTemplateAdapters  doctree.TreeShiftTree[*pagesfromdata.PagesFromTemplate]  // Dynamic pages
}
```

**pageMap Type:**
Referenced throughout code as:
- Location: `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site.go:445`
- Created via: `vrs.pageMap = newPageMap(vrs, memCache, pageTrees)`
- Contains references to:
  - `treePages` - doctree.Tree with all pages
  - `treeResources` - doctree.Tree with all resources
  - `cachePages` - Cache for page queries

**Content Collection:**
- Method: `pagesCollector.Collect()` - Reads files from content directories
- File handling: `AddFi()` method in content_map.go processes individual files
- Path parsing: Uses `paths.PathParser` to categorize content vs resources

**Content Tree Walking:**
Pages are queried via doctree walker patterns:
- `doctree.NodeShiftTreeWalker[contentNode]` walks the trees
- Used in `renderPages()` at `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site_render.go:89-104`

**Key Content Map Operations (content_map.go):**
- `AddFi()` (line 240): Adds file to appropriate tree
- `addPagesFromGoTmplFi()` (line 322): Handles content adapter files
- `getPagesInSection()`: Queries pages below a path
- `getTermsForPageInTaxonomy()`: Gets taxonomy terms for a page

---

### Summary of Key Files

| File | Purpose | Key Types/Functions |
|------|---------|-------------------|
| `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites.go` | HugoSites container | `HugoSites`, sites management |
| `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go` | Build pipeline orchestration | `Build()`, `process()`, `assemble()`, `render()` |
| `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site.go` | Site type and initialization | `Site`, `NewHugoSites()`, `newPageMap()` |
| `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/content_map.go` | Content tree management | `pageMap`, `AddFi()`, content node operations |
| `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/site_render.go` | Page rendering | `renderPages()`, page tree walking |
| `/home/bench/repos/hugo/hugolib/page.go` | Individual page state | `pageState`, page methods |
